  • Generate the cypher query which is supported by SAP HANA Graph Workspace
  • Execute the query and get the answer.

Part 1 : Create Graph Workspace and Upload Data

For detailed explanation please refer this tutorial: Create an SAP HANA Graph Workspace | SAP Tutorials

 

Step 1 : Create two tables for Nodes and Edges of the Graph.

 

CREATE TABLE "ERP_NODES"(
	"NODE_ID"  NVARCHAR(5000) NOT NULL,
	"DESCRIPTION" NVARCHAR(5000),
	"NAME" NVARCHAR(5000),
	"NODE_TYPE" NVARCHAR(5000),
	"NODE_VECTOR" REAL_VECTOR,
	PRIMARY KEY (
		"NODE_ID"
	)
);

CREATE TABLE "ERP_EDGES"(
	"EDGE_ID"  NVARCHAR(5000) NOT NULL,
	"SOURCE" NVARCHAR(5000) NOT NULL,
	"DESTINATION"NVARCHAR(5000) NOT NULL,
	"RELATIONSHIP_TYPE" NVARCHAR(5000),
	"EDGE_VECTOR" REAL_VECTOR,
	PRIMARY KEY (
		"EDGE_ID"
	)
);

 

 

Step 2 :  Create Graph Work space

 

ALTER TABLE "ERP_EDGES" ADD FOREIGN KEY ("SOURCE") REFERENCES "ERP_NODES" ("NODE_ID") ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE ENFORCED VALIDATED;

ALTER TABLE "ERP_EDGES" ADD FOREIGN KEY ( "DESTINATION" ) REFERENCES "ERP_NODES" ("NODE_ID") ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE ENFORCED VALIDATED;

CREATE GRAPH WORKSPACE "ERP"
  EDGE TABLE "ERP_EDGES"
    SOURCE COLUMN "SOURCE"
    TARGET COLUMN "DESTINATION"
    KEY COLUMN "EDGE_ID"
  VERTEX TABLE "ERP_NODES"
    KEY COLUMN "NODE_ID";

 

 

Step 3: Download the data from here

Let's consider the following graph example for our case.

 

This excel contains sheets per node and edge. Node (Entities) sheet depicts Node with NODE_ID and Description.  Edge (relationships) sheet depicts how two nodes are related to each other.

 

Step 4: Upload the data using following code.

CODE:

 

import pandas as pd
from hdbcli import dbapi
import uuid
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.init_models import init_embedding_model
from gen_ai_hub.proxy import GenAIHubProxyClient
import os
from langchain_community.callbacks import get_openai_callback
os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_ID"] = "AICORE_CLIENT_ID"
os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET"] = "AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET"
os.environ["AICORE_AUTH_URL"] = "AICORE_AUTH_URL"
os.environ["AICORE_BASE_URL"] = "AICORE_BASE_URL"
os.environ["AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP"] = "AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP"
gen_ai_hub_proxy_client = GenAIHubProxyClient(
    base_url="AICORE_BASE_URL",
    auth_url="AICORE_AUTH_URL",
    client_id="AICORE_CLIENT_ID",
    client_secret="AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET",
    resource_group="AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP"
)
embeddings = init_embedding_model('text-embedding-ada-002')
HANA_DB_ADDRESS = "HANA_DB_ADDRESS"
HANA_DB_PORT = "HANA_DB_PORT"
HANA_DB_USER = "HANA_DB_USER"
HANA_DB_PASSWORD ="HANA_DB_PASSWORD"
 
driver = dbapi.connect(
    address=HANA_DB_ADDRESS, 
    port=HANA_DB_PORT, 
    user=HANA_DB_USER, 
    password=HANA_DB_PASSWORD
)
cursor = driver.cursor()
# Read data from Excel
excel_file = "data_blog.xlsx"  # Replace with your Excel file path
 
customer_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Customer')
order_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Order')
product_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Product')
supplier_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Supplier')
category_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Category')
customer_order_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Customer_Order')
order_product_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Order_Product')
product_supplier_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Product_Supplier')
product_category_df = pd.read_excel(excel_file, sheet_name='Product_Category')

 
# Function to create nodes
def create_node(cursor, id, description,node_type, node_vector):
    query = 'INSERT INTO ERP_NODES (NODE_ID, DESCRIPTION, NAME, NODE_TYPE, NODE_VECTOR) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, TO_REAL_VECTOR(?))'
    cursor.execute(query, (id, description, description, node_type, node_vector))
 
# Function to create relationships
def create_relationship(cursor, id, source, destination, relationship_type, edge_vector):
    query = 'INSERT INTO ERP_EDGES (EDGE_ID, SOURCE, DESTINATION, RELATIONSHIP_TYPE, EDGE_VECTOR) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, TO_REAL_VECTOR(?))'
    cursor.execute(query, (id, source, destination, relationship_type,edge_vector))
 
# Create Customer nodes
for index, row in customer_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['ID']) and pd.notna(row['Description']):
        node_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query(row['Description']))
        create_node(cursor=cursor, id=row['ID'], description=row['Description'], node_type="Customer",node_vector=node_vector)

# Create Order nodes
for index, row in order_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['ID']) and pd.notna(row['Description']):
        node_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query(row['Description']))
        create_node(cursor=cursor, id=row['ID'], description=row['Description'], node_type="Order",node_vector=node_vector)
        

# Create Product nodes
for index, row in product_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['ID']) and pd.notna(row['Description']):
        node_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query(row['Description']))
        create_node(cursor=cursor, id=row['ID'], description=row['Description'], node_type="Product",node_vector=node_vector)
        

# Create Supplier nodes
for index, row in supplier_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['ID']) and pd.notna(row['Description']):
        node_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query(row['Description']))
        create_node(cursor=cursor, id=row['ID'], description=row['Description'], node_type="Supplier",node_vector=node_vector)
        
# Create Category nodes
for index, row in category_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['ID']) and pd.notna(row['Description']):
        node_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query(row['Description']))
        create_node(cursor=cursor, id=row['ID'], description=row['Description'], node_type="Category",node_vector=node_vector)

# Create relationships between Customer and Order
for index, row in customer_order_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['Customer_ID']) and pd.notna(row['Order_ID']):
        customer_places_order_edge_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query("CUSTOMER_PLACES_ORDER"))
        order_associated_to_customer_edge_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query("ORDER_ASSOCIATED_TO_CUSTOMER"))
        create_relationship(cursor=cursor, id=str(uuid.uuid4()), source=row['Customer_ID'], destination=row['Order_ID'], relationship_type="CUSTOMER_PLACES_ORDER", edge_vector=customer_places_order_edge_vector)
        create_relationship(cursor=cursor, id=str(uuid.uuid4()), source=row['Order_ID'], destination=row['Customer_ID'], relationship_type="ORDER_ASSOCIATED_TO_CUSTOMER", edge_vector=order_associated_to_customer_edge_vector)
        

# Create relationships between Order and Product
for index, row in order_product_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['Order_ID']) and pd.notna(row['Product_ID']):
        order_has_product_edge_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query("ORDER_HAS_PRODUCTS"))
        create_relationship(cursor=cursor, id=str(uuid.uuid4()), source=row['Order_ID'], destination=row['Product_ID'], relationship_type="ORDER_HAS_PRODUCTS", edge_vector=order_has_product_edge_vector)
        

# Create relationships between Product and Supplier
for index, row in product_supplier_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['Product_ID']) and pd.notna(row['Supplier_ID']):
        product_supplied_by_edge_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query("PRODUCT_SUPPLIED_BY"))
        supplier_supplies_product_edge_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query("SUPPLIER_SUPPLIES_PRODUCT"))
        create_relationship(cursor=cursor, id=str(uuid.uuid4()), source=row['Product_ID'], destination=row['Supplier_ID'], relationship_type="PRODUCT_SUPPLIED_BY", edge_vector=product_supplied_by_edge_vector)
        create_relationship(cursor=cursor, id=str(uuid.uuid4()), source=row['Supplier_ID'], destination=row['Product_ID'], relationship_type="SUPPLIER_SUPPLIES_PRODUCT", edge_vector=supplier_supplies_product_edge_vector)
      
# Create relationships between Product and Category
for index, row in product_category_df.iterrows():
    if pd.notna(row['Product_ID']) and pd.notna(row['Category_ID']):
        product_belongs_to_category_edge_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query("PRODUCT_BELONGS_TO_CATEGORY"))
        category_has_products_edge_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query("CATEGORY_HAS_PRODUCTS"))
        create_relationship(cursor=cursor, id=str(uuid.uuid4()), source=row['Product_ID'], destination=row['Category_ID'], relationship_type="PRODUCT_BELONGS_TO_CATEGORY", edge_vector=product_belongs_to_category_edge_vector)
        create_relationship(cursor=cursor, id=str(uuid.uuid4()), source=row['Category_ID'], destination=row['Product_ID'], relationship_type="CATEGORY_HAS_PRODUCTS", edge_vector=category_has_products_edge_vector)

cursor.close()

 

 

Part 2 : Generate cypher query from English language and fetching data from database

LetвАЩs take a look at the problems we will have to address to generate cypher query from English language.

  1. Generate cypher query using Large Language Model
  2. How to identify unspecified data from query

Generate cypher query using Large Language Model

Prompt is helpful in generating cypher query from English language. We will have to provide the rules and schema to the LLM to generate schema.

SCHEMA:

 

[
	{ Node:  
		[  
		{ name:NODE_TYPE, data_type: STRING }
		{ name:DESCRIPTION, data_type: STRING } ,   
		{ name:NODE_ID, data_type: STRING }    
		predefined list of NODE_TYPE:[ 
							Customer, 
							Order,
							Product, 
							Category,   
							Supplier
							]
		]   
	}
]

Relationships: 
[ 
	{ CUSTOMER_PLACES_ORDER: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Customer), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Order) ], description : ' Customer places one or many orders '}, 
	{ ORDER_ASSOCIATED_TO_CUSTOMER: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Order), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Customer) ], description : ' Order is associated with Customer'}, 
	{ ORDER_HAS_PRODUCTS : [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Order), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product) ], description : ' Order contains one or more products '}, 
	{ PRODUCT_SUPPLIED_BY: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Supplier) ], description : ' Product is supplied by one or more suppliers '}, 
	{ SUPPLIER_SUPPLIES_PRODUCT: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Supplier), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product) ], description : ' Product is supplied by one or more suppliers }, 
	{ PRODUCT_BELONGS_TO_CATEGORY: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Category) ], description : ' Product belongs to a specific category '},
	{ CATEGORY_HAS_PRODUCTS: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Category), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product) ], description : ' Category has one or more product '},
]

 

PROMPT:

 

You need to check all the relationships before generating query. Relationships and query may not have exact wordings. 
RULES: 
1. For the generation of open cypher query use node fields NODE_ID and DESCRIPTION in condition of NODE and generate cypher query only using provided relationships and schema.
2. There can be chances where, to reach final output one need to traverse multiple nodes.
3.Output can have multiple nodes.
4. Bidirectional edge Traversal means source node to destination node and destination to source can be traversed. Uni directional edge traversal means only source to destination can be traversed but destination to source cannot be traversed. Please check edge traversal during generating relationships in cypher query
5. DO NOT write Node Type in Match instead give it in WHERE clause.
6. In RETURN statement always give appropriate alias for row.
7. For COUNT and aggregator function always return with attribute of NODE Eg. COUNT(q.NODE_ID).
8. MOST IMPORTANT RULE: Strictly use one or multiple different relations in MATCH clause as shown if needed.\n Eg. \"MATCH (q)-[e1]->(a), (a)-[e2]->(p)\" .
9.Generated Cypher query should follow given schema and specified relationships only.
10. If in query user asks for tasks, return name of tasks. If in query user asks for Qgate, return name of Qgate. If query does not contains any of the node type or relationship type for output node, return \"\".
11. If user asks question(s) replace it with Qgate.
12.If exact data is provided in user question generate query. If data provided is ambigous and  node type for given data is not menyioned do not generate the query, only return unspecified_data. unspecified_data should contain explicitly user defined nodes and conditions in list.
13 Always give relationship value in WHERE clause for every relationship defined in MATCH clause. It should also contain list of data for which user has not provided NODE_TYPE or RELATIONSHIP_TYPE explicitly. Any data which is part of pre defined list of Phase and mentioned in user question, treat it as Phase.Predefined List of phases - [\"Prepare\",\"Deployment(Deploy)\",\"Realize\",\"Run\",\"Execute\"]
14. Do not generate the cypher query if no valid relationships exists between the nodes in provided schema.
15.Always analyze the whole schema and use shortest route and minimum relationships to reach output node.

Example of generated Cypher based on user question:
User question: \"What are the Realise phase Qgate questions for topic extensibility?\".
 The generated cypher should follow this query syntax : 
\" MATCH (p)-[e1]->(t), (t)-[e2]->(q) WHERE (p.DESCRIPTION = 'Realize' OR p.NODE_ID = 'Realize') AND p.NODE_TYPE = 'Phase' AND (t.DESCRIPTION = 'extensibility' OR t.NODE_ID = 'extensibility') AND t.NODE_TYPE = 'Topic' AND q.NODE_TYPE = 'Qgate' AND e1.RELATIONSHIP_TYPE = 'HAS_TOPIC_FROM_PHASE' AND e2.RELATIONSHIP_TYPE = 'HAS_QGATE_FROM_TOPIC' RETURN q.DESCRIPTION AS Questions\". 
Example for output_node:
User Question : \"Get all answers for extensibility\".
Here output_node is \"Answer\". Check which node user is expecting as output. \n\nExample for unspecified data :
User Question : Get all answer for clean core. Here, clean core is ambiguous, so it becomes unspecified_data. output_node is answer. Since clean core is ambiguous, generated cypher is empty and eventually io_node_information is also empty. 
Output :{\"cpyher_query\":\"\"io_node_information\":\"None\", \"unspecified_data\":\"[\"clean core\"]\",\"output_node\":\"Answer\"}
Instructions: DO NOT use any other relationship types or properties that are not provided in following schema. 
Schema: {schema}

The question is: {question}. 

Output Format : {output_format}
Do not include any explanations or details in your responses.

 

This is an example prompt. Prompt needs to be changed depending on need and models used.

OUTPUT FORMAT:

 

{
    "cypher_query": "Only Generated cypher query for questions",
    "io_node_information": "Every WHERE clause attribute used in query as key and value as JSON",
    "unspecified_data": "Data which is ambigous and it's NODE_TYPE can be multiple nodes from schema as python list of string engulfed in double quotes.",
    "output_node": "Return user's final ask which matches with NODE_TYPE in schema or else  return "None"."
}

 

 

How to identify unspecified data from query

LetвАЩs understand the problem first.  Consider we get the query вАЬWhat categories does Monica have?вАЭ or вАЬWhat categories does John Cook have?вАЭ. Here we donвАЩt know whether Monica, John Cook is Customer or Supplier. Now both can have categories of products. John Cook being a customer can have products from some categories in his order while Monica being the supplier she can have products form some categories to supply. To solve this problem, we have unspecified data in OUTPUT FORMAT. Here we ask LLM to identify the data which donвАЩt have node type specified. In this case, John Cook or Monica is unspecified data. Now we make similarity search on unspecified data and find the NODE_TYPE of it and replace unspecified data in query with вАЬNODE_TYPE + unspecified dataвАЭ. So the new query becomes, вАЬWhat categories does Supplier Monica have?вАЭ or вАЬWhat categories does Customer John Cook have?. Now we again make a call to LLM to generate the open cypher query since the query will be more accurate. Also we have io_node_information in OUTPUT_FORMAT. This we will use to get the correct answer even if there are small typos in query. With the given attributes in WHERE Clause we make similarity search and replace the exact word or description available in database as open cypher uses string search instead of similarity search. output_node in OUTPUT FORMAT can be used to generate a nice descriptive answer. We can again use LLM to generate the answer by providing data retrieved from Database, query and output_node.

CODE:

 

import json, re, os
from hdbcli import dbapi
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.init_models import init_embedding_model
from gen_ai_hub.proxy import GenAIHubProxyClient
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.amazon import ChatBedrock
from langchain.schema import AIMessage, HumanMessage, SystemMessage

def replace_where_clause(query, replacement):
    for key, value in replacement.items():
        # Create regex pattern to find the clause to replace
        pattern = rf"({key}\s*=\s*')[^']*(')"
        replacement_string = rf"\1{value}\2"

        # Perform the replacement
        query = re.sub(pattern, replacement_string, query)

    return query

HANA_DB_ADDRESS = "HANA_DB_ADDRESS"
HANA_DB_PORT = "HANA_DB_PORT"
HANA_DB_USER = "HANA_DB_USER"
HANA_DB_PASSWORD ="HANA_DB_PASSWORD"

os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_ID"] = "AICORE_CLIENT_ID"
os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET"] = "AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET"
os.environ["AICORE_AUTH_URL"] = "AICORE_AUTH_URL"
os.environ["AICORE_BASE_URL"] = "AICORE_BASE_URL"
os.environ["AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP"] = "AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP"

gen_ai_hub_proxy_client = GenAIHubProxyClient(
    base_url="AICORE_BASE_URL",
    auth_url="AICORE_AUTH_URL",
    client_id="AICORE_CLIENT_ID",
    client_secret="AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET",
    resource_group="AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP"
)
 
driver = dbapi.connect(
    address=HANA_DB_ADDRESS, 
    port=HANA_DB_PORT, 
    user=HANA_DB_USER, 
    password=HANA_DB_PASSWORD
)

chat_model = ChatBedrock(proxy_client=gen_ai_hub_proxy_client,
                        model_name= "anthropic--claude-3.5-sonnet"
                                     )

cursor = driver.cursor()
schema = """[
                { Node:  
                [  
                { name:NODE_TYPE, data_type: STRING }
                { name:DESCRIPTION, data_type: STRING } ,   
                { name:NODE_ID, data_type: STRING }    
                predefined list of NODE_TYPE:[ 
                                    Customer, 
                                    Order,
                                    Product, 
                                    Category,   
                                    Supplier
                                    ]
                ]   
                }
                ]

                Relationships: 
                [ 
                { CUSTOMER_PLACES_ORDER: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Customer), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Order) ], description : ' Customer places one or many orders '}, 
                { ORDER_ASSOCIATED_TO_CUSTOMER: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Order), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Customer) ], description : ' Order is associated with Customer '}, 

                { ORDER_HAS_PRODUCTS : [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Order), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product) ], description : ' Order contains one or more products '}, 
                { PRODUCT_SUPPLIED_BY: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Supplier) ], description : ' Product is supplied by one or more suppliers '}, 
                { SUPPLIER_SUPPLIES_PRODUCT: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Supplier), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product) ], description : ' Product is supplied by one or more suppliers }, 

                { PRODUCT_BELONGS_TO_CATEGORY: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Category) ], description : ' Product belongs to a specific category '},
                { CATEGORY_HAS_PRODUCTS: [ source: Node(NODE_TYPE: Category), destination: Node(NODE_TYPE: Product) ], description : ' Category has one or more product '},
            ]
"""
output_format = "{\"cypher_query\":\"Only Generated cypher query for questions. If multiple relationship exists, make sure to follow 8th RULE.\",\"io_node_information\":\"Every WHERE clause attribute used in query as key and value as JSON\", \"unspecified_data\":\"Data which is ambigous and it's NODE_TYPE can be multiple nodes from schema as python list of string engulfed in double quotes.\",\"output_node\":\"return user's final ask which matches with NODE_TYPE in schema or else  return \"None\".\"}"
query = "What categories does Monica have?"
question = query
cypher_generation_prompt = f"""Relationships and query may not have exact wordings. RULES: \n1. For the generation of open cypher query use node fields NODE_ID and DESCRIPTION in condition of NODE and generate cypher query only using provided relationships and schema.\n2. There can be chances where, to reach final output one need to traverse multiple nodes.\n3.Output can have multiple nodes.\n4. Bidirectional edge Traversal means source node to destination node and destination to source can be traversed. Uni directional edge traversal means only source to destination can be traversed but destination to source cannot be traversed. Please check edge traversal during generating relationships in cypher query\n5. DO NOT write Node Type in Match instead give it in WHERE clause.\n6. In RETURN statement always give appropriate alias for row.\n7. For COUNT and aggregator function always return with attribute of NODE Eg. COUNT(q.NODE_ID).\n8. MOST IMPORTANT RULE: Strictly use one or multiple different relations in MATCH clause as shown if needed. Do NOT combine two relations even though they are continous.\n Eg. \"MATCH (q)-[e1]->(a), (a)-[e2]->(p)\" .\n9.Generated Cypher query should follow given schema and specified relationships only.\n 10. If in query user asks for tasks, return name of tasks. If in query user asks for Qgate, return name of Qgate. If query does not contains any of the node type or relationship type for output node, return \"\".\n11. If user asks question(s) replace it with Qgate.\n12.If exact data is provided in user question generate query. If data provided is ambigous and  node type for given data is not menyioned do not generate the query, only return unspecified_data. unspecified_data should contain explicitly user defined nodes and conditions in list.\n13 Always give relationship value in WHERE clause for every relationship defined in MATCH clause. It should also contain list of data for which user has not provided NODE_TYPE or RELATIONSHIP_TYPE explicitly. Any data which is part of pre defined list of Phase and mentioned in user question, treat it as Phase.Predefined List of phases - [\"Prepare\",\"Deployment(Deploy)\",\"Realize\",\"Run\",\"Execute\"]\n14. Do not generate the cypher query if no valid relationships exists between the nodes in provided schema.\n15.Always analyze the whole schema and use shortest route and minimum relationships to reach output node.\n\nExample of generated Cypher based on user question:\nUser question: \"What are the Realise phase Qgate questions for topic extensibility?\".\n The generated cypher should follow this query syntax : \" MATCH (p)-[e1]->(t), (t)-[e2]->(q) WHERE (p.DESCRIPTION = 'Realize' OR p.NODE_ID = 'Realize') AND p.NODE_TYPE = 'Phase' AND (t.DESCRIPTION = 'extensibility' OR t.NODE_ID = 'extensibility') AND t.NODE_TYPE = 'Topic' AND q.NODE_TYPE = 'Qgate' AND e1.RELATIONSHIP_TYPE = 'HAS_TOPIC_FROM_PHASE' AND e2.RELATIONSHIP_TYPE = 'HAS_QGATE_FROM_TOPIC' RETURN q.DESCRIPTION AS Questions\". \n\nExample for output_node:\nUser Question : \"Get all answers for extensibility\".\n Here output_node is \"Answer\". Check which node user is expecting as output. \n\nExample for unspecified data :\nUser Question : Which products are associated with Michael Brown?.Here, Michael Brown is ambiguous, so it becomes unspecified_data as we don't know whether Michael Brown is Supplier or Customer. output_node is answer. Since Michael Brown is ambiguous, generated cypher is empty and eventually io_node_information is also empty. Output :\"cpyher_query\":\"\"io_node_information\":\"None\", \"unspecified_data\":\"[\"Michael Brown\"]\",\"ouput_node\":\"Answer\"Instructions:\n 1.DO NOT use any other relationship types or properties that are not provided in following schema.\n2.Do not assume NODE_TYPE of any data in question while generating open cypher query following above RULES. If NODE_TYPE is not mentioned in question, STRICTLY identify that data is unspecified_data.\n3.If no unspecified data then follow RULE no 8, STRICTLY.\nSchema: {schema}\n\nThe question is: {question}. \n\n\nOutput Format : {output_format}\nDo not include any explanations or details in your responses. The output shoud be parsable json object which can be passed to json.loads() in python."""
batch_messages = [
            [
                HumanMessage(content="You are an AI assistant which generates open cypher query using the given schema and its relations only if relation_ship exists between them.While generating query you double check it. Generate Open Cypher statement to query a SAP HANA graph workspace."),
                AIMessage(content= "Provide me the RULES and user question."),
                HumanMessage(content=cypher_generation_prompt)
            ]
    ]
resp = chat_model.generate(batch_messages)
resp = json.loads(resp.model_dump_json())
cypher_query_prompt_response = resp['generations'][0][0]["message"]["content"]
embeddings = init_embedding_model('text-embedding-ada-002')
# cursor = self.db.cursor()

response = json.loads(cypher_query_prompt_response)
attributes = {}
is_where_clause_replacement_needed = True
node_sql_query = f"SELECT TOP 1 *, COSINE_SIMILARITY(NODE_VECTOR, TO_REAL_VECTOR(?)) AS SIMILARITY FROM ERP_NODES ORDER BY COSINE_SIMILARITY(NODE_VECTOR, TO_REAL_VECTOR(?)) DESC;"
if type(response["unspecified_data"]) == str:
    response["unspecified_data"] = json.loads(response["unspecified_data"])

#User query has unspecified data.
if len(response["unspecified_data"]) > 0:
    for entry in response["unspecified_data"]:
        entry_CAPS = entry.upper()
        node_id_query = """SELECT TOP 1 * FROM ERP_NODES WHERE NODE_ID = ?"""
        cursor.execute(node_id_query, (entry_CAPS))
        query_res = cursor.fetchall()
        # Check if unspecified data has matching NODE_ID from our system
        if len(query_res) != 0:
            replacement = str(query_res[0][3]) + " " + str(query_res[0][0])
            # Replace unspecified ID with it's nodetype
            query = query.replace(entry, replacement)
        else:
            # Check if unspecified data has matching Description from our system
            entry_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query(entry))
            cursor.execute(node_sql_query, (entry_vector, entry_vector))
            node_sql_query_response = cursor.fetchall()
            if float(node_sql_query_response[0][5]) > 0.88:
                temp = ""
                temp = node_sql_query_response[0][3] + " " + node_sql_query_response[0][2]
                 # Replace unspecified Description with it's nodetype
                query = query.replace(entry, temp)
    new_question = query
    batch_messages = [
            [
                SystemMessage(content="You are an AI assistant which generates open cypher query using the given schema and its relations only if relation_ship exists between them.While generating query you double check it. Generate Open Cypher statement to query a SAP HANA graph workspace."),
                AIMessage(content= "Provide me the RULES and user question."),
                HumanMessage(content=f"""Relationships and query may not have exact wordings. RULES: \n1. For the generation of open cypher query use node fields NODE_ID and DESCRIPTION in condition of NODE and generate cypher query only using provided relationships and schema.\n2. There can be chances where, to reach final output one need to traverse multiple nodes.\n3.Output can have multiple nodes.\n4. Bidirectional edge Traversal means source node to destination node and destination to source can be traversed. Uni directional edge traversal means only source to destination can be traversed but destination to source cannot be traversed. Please check edge traversal during generating relationships in cypher query\n5. DO NOT write Node Type in Match instead give it in WHERE clause.\n6. In RETURN statement always give appropriate alias for row.\n7. For COUNT and aggregator function always return with attribute of NODE Eg. COUNT(q.NODE_ID).\n8. MOST IMPORTANT RULE: Strictly use one or multiple different relations in MATCH clause as shown if needed. Do NOT combine two relations even though they are continous.\n Eg. \"MATCH (q)-[e1]->(a), (a)-[e2]->(p)\" .\n9.Generated Cypher query should follow given schema and specified relationships only.\n 10. If in query user asks for tasks, return name of tasks. If in query user asks for Qgate, return name of Qgate. If query does not contains any of the node type or relationship type for output node, return \"\".\n11. If user asks question(s) replace it with Qgate.\n12.If exact data is provided in user question generate query. If data provided is ambigous and  node type for given data is not menyioned do not generate the query, only return unspecified_data. unspecified_data should contain explicitly user defined nodes and conditions in list.\n13 Always give relationship value in WHERE clause for every relationship defined in MATCH clause. It should also contain list of data for which user has not provided NODE_TYPE or RELATIONSHIP_TYPE explicitly. Any data which is part of pre defined list of Phase and mentioned in user question, treat it as Phase.Predefined List of phases - [\"Prepare\",\"Deployment(Deploy)\",\"Realize\",\"Run\",\"Execute\"]\n14. Do not generate the cypher query if no valid relationships exists between the nodes in provided schema.\n15.Always analyze the whole schema and use shortest route and minimum relationships to reach output node.\n\nExample of generated Cypher based on user question:\nUser question: \"What are the Realise phase Qgate questions for topic extensibility?\".\n The generated cypher should follow this query syntax : \" MATCH (p)-[e1]->(t), (t)-[e2]->(q) WHERE (p.DESCRIPTION = 'Realize' OR p.NODE_ID = 'Realize') AND p.NODE_TYPE = 'Phase' AND (t.DESCRIPTION = 'extensibility' OR t.NODE_ID = 'extensibility') AND t.NODE_TYPE = 'Topic' AND q.NODE_TYPE = 'Qgate' AND e1.RELATIONSHIP_TYPE = 'HAS_TOPIC_FROM_PHASE' AND e2.RELATIONSHIP_TYPE = 'HAS_QGATE_FROM_TOPIC' RETURN q.DESCRIPTION AS Questions\". \n\nExample for output_node:\nUser Question : \"Get all answers for extensibility\".\n Here output_node is \"Answer\". Check which node user is expecting as output. \n\nExample for unspecified data :\nUser Question : Which products are associated with Michael Brown?.Here, Michael Brown is ambiguous, so it becomes unspecified_data as we don't know whether Michael Brown is Supplier or Customer. output_node is answer. Since Michael Brown is ambiguous, generated cypher is empty and eventually io_node_information is also empty. Output :\"cpyher_query\":\"\"io_node_information\":\"None\", \"unspecified_data\":\"[\"Michael Brown\"]\",\"ouput_node\":\"Answer\"Instructions:\n 1.DO NOT use any other relationship types or properties that are not provided in following schema.\nSchema: {schema}\n\nThe question is: {new_question}. \n\n\nOutput Format : {output_format}\nDo not include any explanations or details in your responses. The output shoud be parsable json object which can be passed to json.loads() in python.""")
            ]
    ]
    # Again make the LLM call to generate the cypher query but with more enhanced data.
    resp = chat_model.generate(batch_messages)
    resp = json.loads(resp.model_dump_json())
    response = resp['generations'][0][0]["message"]["content"]

    response = json.loads(response)
    is_where_clause_replacement_needed = False

else:
    # If there is no unspcified data, make sure that whatever present in WHERE clause of generated cypher query has 
    # correct details as it STRING search to database.
    io_node_information = response["io_node_information"]
    if type(io_node_information) == str:
        io_node_information = io_node_information.replace("'",'"')
        io_node_information = json.loads(io_node_information)
    for key, value in io_node_information.items():
        val = value
        if "DESCRIPTION" in key:
            node_sql_query = f"SELECT TOP 1 *, COSINE_SIMILARITY(NODE_VECTOR, TO_REAL_VECTOR(?)) AS SIMILARITY FROM ERP_NODES ORDER BY COSINE_SIMILARITY(NODE_VECTOR, TO_REAL_VECTOR(?)) DESC;"
            value_vector = str(embeddings.embed_query(value))
            cursor.execute(node_sql_query, (value_vector, value_vector))
            attribute_result = cursor.fetchall()
            if float(attribute_result[0][5]) > 0.88:
                val = attribute_result[0][2]
        attributes.update({key: val})
if is_where_clause_replacement_needed:
    final_cypher_query = replace_where_clause(response["cypher_query"], attributes)
else:
    final_cypher_query = response["cypher_query"]
output_node = response["output_node"]


#Making a Graph Workspace query and retrieving the answer.
if final_cypher_query == '':
    print("Query not generated")
else:
    final_cypher_query = final_cypher_query.replace("'", "''")
    final_sql_query = f"""SELECT * FROM OPENCYPHER_TABLE( GRAPH WORKSPACE "DBADMIN"."ERP" QUERY ' {final_cypher_query} ');"""
    cursor.execute(final_sql_query)
    sql_query_response = cursor.fetchall()
    if len(sql_query_response) == 0:
        print("No data found")
    else:
        db_data = ""
        for row in sql_query_response:
            db_data = db_data + "-"
            db_data = db_data + str(row[0])
            db_data = db_data + "\n"

        final_response = db_data
        print(final_response)

 

 

NOTE: The prompt needs little modification depending on Large Language Model which is being used. The current prompt holds the ground with model provided by Claude AI (Model : anthropic--claude-3.5-sonnet).

entry.title: Business AI in SAP procurement
entry.content:
Procurement Efficiency and Compliance with AI

Chief Procurement Officers (CPOs) are facing growing challenges. Rising geopolitical uncertainty, price inflation, new regulations, and a tightening labour market are making it difficult to contain costs, enforce compliance and manage supplier risks. Traditional ways of running the business no longer suffice, and business expectations are rising.

To respond to these challenges Procurement leaders must adopt modern technology to gain greater visibility, improve buying experiences, and increase efficiencies across the entire source-to-pay process.

 

Take the complexity out of the procurement process

With SAP AI, engaging suppliers, selecting goods and services, enforcing compliance, and performing receiving and reconciliation become easier. This AI is embedded into the SAP applications that power the source-to-pay business process, making it more straightforward and cost-effective for procurement organizations to modernize.

AI can profoundly impact all stages of the source-to-pay process. It can help category managers and strategic buyers streamline tasks ranging from identifying suppliers to managing contracts. It can simplify the buying
experience for operational buyers and employees to increase compliance. It can also support accounts payable personnel with intelligent automation that expedites processing across the invoice-to-pay process.

 

Why Partner with SAP Business AI for an AI-powered Procurement

By partnering with SAP, companies can modernize every aspect of your source-to-pay process with AI-powered insights, recommendations, and automation built into SAP applications. It supports Procurement, which is under intense pressure to deliver cost savings, mitigate risk, improve sustainability, and play a more strategic role in the business, to keep up with the rapid pace of change, be more agile and proactive, in everything they do.

SAP Business AI can help in the following key areas of procurement:

 

  • Spend classification and analysis: Spend classification algorithms can rapidly search through line items and highlight keywords to tie to spend categories with near-perfect accuracy. AI-powered spend analysis can also help teams proactively identify opportunities for cost savings and form the basis for better sourcing, category, and spend management strategies.

 

  • Global sourcing strategy: By analyzing large global datasets, machine learning algorithms can identify shifts in supply trends, predict future developments, and help inform global sourcing strategies.

 

  • Guided buying: AI-assisted item recommendations bring together procurement policies to make it simple for users to find what they're looking for, encourage spending within the company's catalog to avoid unnecessary costs, and let the procurement department offer tailored help. It also provides quick access to preferred suppliers while incorporating helpful guardrails.

 

 

  • Intelligent sourcing and supplier management: AI-powered software can analyze supplier databases, market trends, historical data, ESG reports, and other factors to recommend the best suppliers for specific needs. It can also provide comprehensive insight into a companyвАЩs supply base, helping to improve supplier performance and advance strategic priorities.

 

  • RFX creation: AI can automatically generate request for proposals, (RFPs), request for quotations (RFQs), and other RF documentsвАФfrom developing supplier lists to drafting key questions.

 

  • Supplier risk management: AI algorithms can rapidly detect sudden changes with a supplier or vendor and assess how that change will impact risk. They can also mine millions of different data sources to alert companies to potential risk across the supply chain.

 

  • Compliance: By using AI to structure contract, invoice, and PO data, companies can automatically compare payment terms, eliminate duplication, and identify non-compliance.

 

  • Data extraction: Natural language processing can extract data from invoices and contracts to identify risk and fraud, provide greater insight into business spend, and speed up processes end-to-end. NLP can also capture data from external sources such as market indices, company credit ratings, social media, and publicly available information about suppliers to spot opportunities and risk.

 

  • Contract lifecycle management: The AI-powered digital assistant Joule can automatically generate first drafts of contracts, support negotiation, and flag potential risks in contract language. You can also monitor terms and conditions as well as deadlines to ensure compliance.

 

  • Accounts Payable automation: Intelligent RPA can eliminate manual tasks in accounts payable processes, speeding up invoice processing and approvals, improving accuracy, and ensuring compliance. Optical character recognition can read key information off paper-based invoices to improve the process and digitize documents.

 

SAP AI Foundation and Intelligent Technologies in SAP Procurement

  • SAP's AI Foundation is the basis for AI capabilities that SAP embeds across its portfolio also in the Area of Spend Management. It is SAPвАЩs all-in-one AI toolkit, offering Partners and Customers AI thatвАЩs ready-to-use, customizable, grounded in business data, and supported by leading generative AI foundation models. 

 

 

Intelligent Technologies in SAP procurement

There are main intelligent technology types of AI used in SAP procurement today:

  1. Artificial intelligence (AI) Self-learning bots and applications with dynamic adaptability, using AI and generative AI
  2. Machine learning (ML) a subset of AI, machine learning algorithms can recognize patterns in data sets and use them to make decisions, forecasts, or predictions 
  3. Robotic process automation (RPA): algorithms that mimic human actions to perform repetitive tasks. RPA is not technically considered to be a type of AI but can be powered by it.
  4. Natural language processing (NLP): algorithms that can understand, interpret, and generate human language, such as SAP's virtual assistant Joule
  5. Situation Handling: Detect exceptions, notify, and provide insight-to-action options
  6. Optical character recognition (OCR): algorithms that can recognize and extract text from images and scanned documents, such as paper-based invoices

 

Benefits of SAP AI Technologies in sourcing and procurement

The integration of SAP AI in sourcing and procurement-related processes offers many benefits, including:

  • Smarter decision-making: AI can analyze large volumes of data quickly and accurately. This data-driven approach provides procurement professionals with actionable insights into spending patterns, supplier performance and market trends. AI-powered predictive analytics and scenario analysis can also help teams assess options, mitigate risk, and make better sourcing and spend decisions.
  • Efficiency and automation: AI-powered automation of repetitive and time-consuming tasks, such as data entry and invoice processing, improves efficiency and frees up procurement professionals to focus on more strategic work.
  • Cost savings: With AI, organizations can improve supplier selection, negotiate better deals, and more accurately forecast demand, for significant cost savings. They can also analyze spending patterns to identify, and act on, additional opportunities to cut costs.
  • Risk mitigation: AI tools can proactively identify and assess risks associated with suppliers, market conditions, and regulatory changes, so procurement teams can mitigate supply chain disruption before it happens.
  • Better relationships with suppliers: By clearly stating requirements and expectations in requests for proposals, and by monitoring and evaluating supplier performance, AI can contribute to the development of stronger, more reliable supplier relationships.

The result is greater business efficiency and lower risk вАУ whether you procure direct or indirect materials, services, or contingent labor.

entry.title: [жЧ•жЬђи™ЮеМЦ] Generative AI with SAP - Part4 - SAP BTPгБЂгБКгБСгВЛAI Foundation
entry.content:
гБУгБЃгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБѓEcosystem GrowthжіїеЛХгБЃдЄАзТ∞гБІгАМGenerative AI with SAPпЉИ@Raja_GuptaпЉЙгАНгВЈгГ™гГЉгВЇгВТжЧ•жЬђи™ЮеМЦгГїеК†з≠ЖгБЧгБЯгВВгБЃгБІгБЩгАВгБУгБЃгВЈгГ™гГЉгВЇгБІгБѓгАБSAPгБМгГЭгГЉгГИгГХгВ©гГ™гВ™еЕ®дљУгБІгБ©гБЃгВИгБЖгБЂ AI гБКгВИгБ≥ Generative AI гВТжіїзФ®гБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛгБЛгВТзРЖиІ£гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБЂзД¶зВєгВТељУгБ¶гБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБУгБЃгВЈгГ™гГЉгВЇгБІе≠¶гБєгВЛгБУгБ®пЉЪ

  • SAPгБМгГЭгГЉгГИгГХгВ©гГ™гВ™еЕ®дљУгБІ AI гБКгВИгБ≥ Generative AI гВТгБ©гБЃгВИгБЖгБЂжіїзФ®гБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛгБЛ
  • SAP Business AI гБ®гБѓдљХгБЛгАБгБЭгБЧгБ¶SAPгБЃгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉгВДй°ІеЃҐгБМгБЭгВМгВТгБ©гБЃгВИгБЖгБЂжіїзФ®гБІгБНгВЛгБЃгБЛ
  • SAP AI CoreгГїSAP AI LaunchpadгГїJoule гБ®гБѓдљХгБЛ
  • SAPгБЃAIжИ¶зХ•гБЂгБКгБДгБ¶SAP BTPгБМжЮЬгБЯгБЩељєеЙ≤
  • еЃЯйЪЫгБЃгГ¶гГЉгВєгВ±гГЉгВєгВДгГПгГ≥гВЇгВ™гГ≥дЇЛдЊЛгБ™гБ©

зРЖиІ£гБЧгВДгБЩгБПгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгАБгБУгБЃгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гВЈгГ™гГЉгВЇгБѓзЯ≠гБДгВїгВѓгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЂеИЖгБСгВЙгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВгБЭгВМгБЮгВМгБЃгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБѓгАБ10пљЮ15еИЖз®ЛеЇ¶гБІе≠¶зњТгБІгБНгВЛеЖЕеЃєгБІгБЩгАВ

  • Part1 - SAP Business AIгБЃж¶Ви¶Б
  • Part2 - SAP Business AIгБЃжІЛжИРи¶Бзі†
  • Part3 - JouleпЉЪSAPгБЃGenerative AI Copilot
  • Part4 - SAP BTPгБЂгБКгБСгВЛAI Foundation [зПЊеЬ®гБЃгГЦгГ≠гВ∞]
  • Part5 - SAP AI CoreгБЃж¶Ви¶Б
  • Part6 - SAP AI LaunchpadгБЃж¶Ви¶Б
  • Part7 - SAP AI CoreгБ®SAP AI LaunchpadгБЃдљњгБДжЦє
  • Part8 - SAP AI CoreгБЂгБКгБСгВЛHello World
  • Part9 - Generative AI HubгБЃж¶Ви¶Б
  • Part10 - SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
  • Part11 - RAGпЉИRetrieval-Augmented GenerationпЉЙгГїSAP AI CoreгГїGenerative AI HubгГїHANA Cloud vector engine гВТзФ®гБДгБЯеЃЯйЪЫгБЃгГ¶гГЉгВєгВ±гГЉгВє

гБУгБЃгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБѓгВЈгГ™гГЉгВЇгБЃзђђ4еЫЮзЫЃгБІгБВгВКгАБAI Foundation гБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶е≠¶гБ≥гБЊгБЩгАВ

Note: еЊМзґЪгБЃгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гВВй†Жжђ°еЕђйЦЛдЇИеЃЪгБІгБЩгАВ

 

 

гБЊгБЪгБѓ SAP BTPгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶з∞°еНШгБЂгБКгБХгВЙгБДгБЧгБЊгБЧгВЗгБЖ

2021еєі1жЬИгАБSAPгБѓгАМSAP Business Technology PlatformпЉИSAP BTPпЉЙ гАНгВТзЩЇи°®гБЧгБЊгБЧгБЯгАВSAP BTPгБѓгАБSAPгБЃгВЈгВєгГЖгГ†еЕ®дљУгВТжФѓгБИгВЛжКАи°УеЯЇзЫ§гБІгБВгВКгАБй°ІеЃҐгВДгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉгБЂгБ®гБ£гБ¶жђ†гБЛгБЫгБ™гБДе≠ШеЬ®гБІгБЩгАВ

з∞°еНШгБЂи®АгБИгБ∞гАБSAP BTPгБѓжІШгАЕгБ™ SAPгВљгГ™гГ•гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВДгВµгГЉгГУгВєгВТдЄАгБ§гБЂгБЊгБ®гВБгБЯгГЧгГ©гГГгГИгГХгВ©гГЉгГ†гБІгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБЂгВИгВКгАБдЉБж•≠гБѓжЦ∞гБЧгБДгВѓгГ©гВ¶гГЙгВљгГ™гГ•гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВТжІЛзѓЙгБЧгБЯгВКгАБжЧҐе≠ШгБЃ SAPгВЈгВєгГЖгГ†гВТжЯФиїЯгБЂжЛ°еЉµгБЧгБЯгВКгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

Note: и©≥гБЧгБПзЯ•гВКгБЯгБДжЦєгБѓгАМExplaining SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to a BeginnerгАНгВТеПВиАГгБЂгБЧгБ¶гБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

 

SAP BTPгБІ AI гВТз∞°еНШгБЂжіїзФ®гБЩгВЛжЦєж≥ХгБ®гБѓпЉЯ

SAPгБЃй°ІеЃҐгВДгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉдЉБж•≠гБІйЦЛзЩЇгВТжЛЕељУгБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛгБ®гАБжђ°гБЃгВИгБЖгБ™и™≤й°МгБЂзЫійЭҐгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБВгВЛгБЛгВВгБЧгВМгБЊгБЫгВУпЉЪ

  • SAP BTPдЄКгБІжЦ∞гБЧгБДгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВТдљЬгВЛгАБгБЊгБЯгБѓжЧҐе≠ШгВЈгВєгГЖгГ†гВТжЛ°еЉµгБЩгВЛењЕи¶БгБМгБВгВЛ
  • гВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЂ AIж©ЯиГљгВТзµДгБњиЊЉгБњгБЯгБД
  • гВµгГЉгГЙгГСгГЉгГЖгВ£гБЃ AIгГЩгГ≥гГАгГЉгВДе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгГЧгГ≠гГРгВ§гГАгГЉгБ®зЫіжО•е•СзіДгБЫгБЪгБЂгАБз∞°еНШгБЛгБ§еКєзОЗзЪДгБЂе∞ОеЕ•гБЧгБЯгБД

гБУгБЖгБЧгБЯи™≤й°МгВТиІ£ж±ЇгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЂ SAPгБМжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛгБЃгБМгАБAI Foundation гБІгБЩгАВ

 

AI FoundationгБ®гБѓпЉЯгБЭгБЃж¶Ви¶БгБ®жіїзФ®жЦєж≥Х

AI FoundationгБѓгАБSAP BTPдЄКгБЂзФ®жДПгБХгВМгБЯгАМгБЩгБРгБЂдљњгБИгВЛAIгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБ®гГДгГЉгГЂгАНгБЃгВїгГГгГИгБІгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБЂгВИгВКгАБдї•дЄЛгБЃгБУгБ®гБМеПѓиГљгБЂгБ™гВКгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • дњ°й†ЉжАІгБМйЂШгБПгАБеЃЙеЕ®гБІгВєгВ±гГЉгГ©гГЦгГЂгБ™жЦєж≥ХгБІгАБAIж©ЯиГљгВТгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЂзµДгБњиЊЉгБњеПѓиГљ
  • й°ІеЃҐгВДгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉдЉБж•≠гБМжЦ∞гБЧгБД AIжЛ°еЉµж©ЯиГљгВДгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВТз∞°еНШгБЂдљЬжИР
  • гВµгГЉгГЙгГСгГЉгГЖгВ£е§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБ®гБЃеАЛеИ•е•СзіДгВТйБњгБСгАБжЙЛйЦУгВТжЬАе∞ПеМЦ

 

гБ§гБЊгВКгАБAI FoundationгВТдљњгБЖгБУгБ®гБІгАБйЦЛзЩЇиАЕгБѓгВИгВКињЕйАЯгБЛгБ§еКєзОЗзЪДгБЂ AIгВТжіїзФ®гБІгБНгВЛгБЃгБІгБЩгАВ
дї•дЄЛгБЃеЫ≥гБѓгАБAI FoundationгБЃеЕ®дљУеГПгВТз§ЇгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

SAP BTPдЄКгБЃAI FoundationгБѓгАБй°ІеЃҐгГїгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉеРСгБСгБЃжКљи±°еМЦгГђгВ§гГ§гГЉгВТжПРдЊЫ

SAPгБѓгБДгБПгБ§гБЛгБЃAIгГЩгГ≥гГАгГЉгВДе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБЃгГЧгГ≠гГРгВ§гГАгГЉгБ®жПРжРЇгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩпљ°гБУгВМгБЂгВИгВКпљ§й°ІеЃҐгБМгВµгГЉгГЙгГСгГЉгГЖгВ£дЉБж•≠гБ®гБЃеАЛеИ•е•СзіДгВТзµРгБґгБУгБ®гБ™гБПгАБAIгБЂгВИгВЛгВ§гГОгГЩгГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЃжБ©жБµгВТеПЧгБСгВЙгВМгВЛзТ∞еҐГгВТжХігБИгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБЯгБ®гБИгБ∞гАБGPTгВЈгГ™гГЉгВЇгБ™гБ©гБЃе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгВТжіїзФ®гБЧгБЯгБДе†іеРИгАБгВПгБЦгВПгБЦеИ•гБЃгГ©гВ§гВїгГ≥гВєгВТи≥ЉеЕ•гБЩгВЛењЕи¶БгБѓгБВгВКгБЊгБЫгВУгАВSAP BTPдЄКгБЃгАМGenerative AI HubгАНгВТйАЪгБШгБ¶гАБи§ЗжХ∞гБЃгГЩгГ≥гГАгГЉгБМжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЄз∞°еНШгБЂгВҐгВѓгВїгВєгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

AI FoundationгВТжІЛжИРгБЩгВЛдЄїи¶БгВ≥гГ≥гГЭгГЉгГНгГ≥гГИ

дї•дЄЛгБЃеЫ≥гБѓгАБAI FoundationгВТжІЛжИРгБЩгВЛдЄїгБ™и¶Бзі†гВТз§ЇгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБЭгВМгБІгБѓгАБгБУгВМгВЙгБЃгВ≥гГ≥гГЭгГЉгГНгГ≥гГИгВТдЄАгБ§гБ≤гБ®гБ§и©≥гБЧгБПи¶ЛгБ¶гБДгБНгБЊгБЧгВЗгБЖгАВ

 

1. AI Business Services

AI Business ServicesгБѓгАБSAP BTPдЄКгБІжПРдЊЫгБХгВМгВЛгАБгБЩгБРгБЂеИ©зФ®еПѓиГљгБ™ж±ОзФ®AIгВµгГЉгГУгВєзЊ§гБІгБЩгАВ
дї•дЄЛгБЃгВИгБЖгБ™зЙєеЊігБМгБВгВКгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • AI Business ServicesгБЃжіїзФ®гБЂгВИгБ£гБ¶гАБиЗ™з§ЊгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЄгБЃAIж©ЯиГљзµДгБњиЊЉгБњгВТз∞°еНШгБЂеЃЯзПЊ
  • гГУгВЄгГНгВєгБЂйЦҐйА£гБЧгБЯгГЗгГЉгВњгБІдЇЛеЙНе≠¶зњТгБХгВМгБЯAIгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЂгВИгБ£гБ¶гАБеєЕеЇГгБДгГУгВЄгГНгВєгВЈгГКгГ™гВ™гБЂеѓЊењЬеПѓиГљ

дї•дЄЛгБЃеЫ≥гБМз§ЇгБЩгВИгБЖгБЂгАБAI Business ServicesгБѓе§ІгБНгБП3гБ§гБЃгВЂгГЖгВігГ™гБЂеИЖгБСгВЙгВМгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

Document Processing

гВ§гГ≥гГЖгГ™гВЄгВІгГ≥гГИгБ™Document ProcessingгВТеПѓиГљгБЂгБЩгВЛгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБ®гБЧгБ¶гАБгАМDocument Information ExtractionгАНгБМгБВгВКгБЊгБЩгАВ

Document Information Extraction гБЂгБѓгАБдї•дЄЛгБЃгВИгБЖгБ™зЙєеЊігБМгБВгВКгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • ж©ЯжҐ∞е≠¶зњТгВТжіїзФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБжЫЄй°ЮгБЛгВЙгБЃжГЕе†±жКљеЗЇгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгВТиЗ™еЛХеМЦгБЧеКєзОЗеМЦгВТжФѓжПі
  • гГШгГГгГАгГЉгВДи°®ељҐеЉПгБІжХізРЖгБХгВМгБЯжГЕе†±гБМеРЂгБЊгВМгВЛе§ІйЗПгБЃгГУгВЄгГНгВєжЦЗжЫЄгВТгАБеКєжЮЬзЪДгБЛгБ§гВєгГ†гГЉгВЇгБЂеЗ¶зРЖ

гБУгБЃгВµгГЉгГУгВєгВТеИ©зФ®гБЩгВМгБ∞гАБдї•дЄЛгБЃгВИгБЖгБ™еКєжЮЬгБМжЬЯеЊЕгБІгБНгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • е§ІйЗПгБЃжЫЄй°ЮгВТгВИгВКж≠£зҐЇгБЛгБ§еКєзОЗзЪДгБЂеЗ¶зРЖгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБЂгВИгВЛдЇЇзЪДгВ®гГ©гГЉгВДжЙЛйЦУгБЃеЙКжЄЫ
  • еУБи≥™гВДгВ≥гГ≥гГЧгГ©гВ§гВҐгГ≥гВєгВТеЉЈеМЦгБЧгБ§гБ§гАБгГУгВЄгГНгВєжЦЗжЫЄйЦҐйА£гБЃж•≠еЛЩгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгВТжФєеЦД

жКљеЗЇгБЧгБЯжГЕе†±гБѓгАБгБХгВЙгБ™гВЛгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБЃиЗ™еЛХеМЦгБЂжіїзФ®еПѓиГљгБІгБЩгАВгБЯгБ®гБИгБ∞гАБиЂЛж±ВжЫЄгВДжФѓжЙХгБДдЊЭй†ЉжЫЄгВТиЗ™еЛХзЪДгБЂеЗ¶зРЖгБЧгАБиЂЛж±Вй°НгБ®жФѓжЙХй°НгВТжХіеРИгБХгБЫгВЛгБ™гБ©гАБж•≠еЛЩгВТдЄАе±§еЖЖжїСгБЂйА≤гВБгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

RecommendationпЉИгГђгВ≥гГ°гГ≥гГЗгГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥пЉЙ

гБУгБЃгВЂгГЖгВігГ™гБІгБѓгАБдЇЇйЦУгБЂгВИгВЛжДПжАЭж±ЇеЃЪгВТжФѓжПігГїеЉЈеМЦгБЩгВЛдї•дЄЛ3гБ§гБЃгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБМзФ®жДПгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • Personalized RecommendationпЉИгГСгГЉгВљгГКгГ©гВ§гВЇгГЙгГїгГђгВ≥гГ°гГ≥гГЗгГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥пЉЙ
  • Data Attribute RecommendationпЉИгГЗгГЉгВње±ЮжАІгГђгВ≥гГ°гГ≥гГЗгГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥пЉЙ
  • Business Entity RecognitionпЉИгГУгВЄгГНгВєгВ®гГ≥гГЖгВ£гГЖгВ£и™Ни≠ШпЉЙ

 

Personalized Recommendation гБѓгАБжІШгАЕгБ™ж•≠еЛЩгВДгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБІзє∞гВКињФгБЧжіїзФ®гБІгБНгВЛжЯФиїЯгБ™гВµгГЉгГУгВєгБІгБЩгАВдї•дЄЛгБЃгВИгБЖгБ™зЙєеЊігБМгБВгВКгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • ж©ЯжҐ∞е≠¶зњТгВТзФ®гБДгБ¶гАБй°ІеЃҐгБЃйЦ≤и¶Іе±•ж≠ігВДеХЖеУБи™ђжШОгБЂеЯЇгБ•гБДгБ¶еАЛеИ•гБЂгГђгВ≥гГ°гГ≥гГЗгГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩ
  • еєЕеЇГгБДгГУгВЄгГНгВєгВЈгГКгГ™гВ™гБЂеѓЊењЬгБІгБНгВЛгВИгБЖе≠¶зњТжЄИгБњгГҐгГЗгГЂгВТгГИгГђгГЉгГЛгГ≥гВ∞гБЧгАБйБ©зФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩ

гБЯгБ®гБИгБ∞гАБSAPгБѓ Personalized Recommendation гВТжіїзФ®гБЧгАБSAP SuccessFactors гБЃй°ІеЃҐеРСгБСгБЂгАБжѓОжЬИ420дЄЗдїґдї•дЄКгБЃе≠¶зњТгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгГ≥гГДгВТжО®е•®гБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБЂгВИгВКгАБгГ¶гГЉгВґгГЉгБѓгВИгВКйБ©еИЗгБ™гВ≥гГ≥гГЖгГ≥гГДгБЂињЕйАЯгБЂгВҐгВѓгВїгВєгБІгБНгАБе≠¶зњТдљУй®УгБЃеРСдЄКгВТеЃЯзПЊгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

Data Attribute Recommendation гБѓгАБгГЗгГЉгВњгВїгГГгГИеЖЕгБЂжђ†жРНеА§гБМгБВгВЛе†іеРИгБЂжіїзФ®гБІгБНгВЛгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБІгБЩгАВгБУгБЃгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБѓгАБдї•дЄЛгБЃгВИгБЖгБ™зЙєеЊігБМгБВгВКгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • иЗ™зДґи®Аи™ЮгГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгАБжХ∞еА§гАБгВЂгГЖгВігГ™жГЕе†±гВТеЕ•еКЫгБ®гБЧгБ¶дљњзФ®гБЧгБЊгБЩ
  • и£љеУБгГїеЇЧиИЧгГїгГ¶гГЉгВґгГЉгБ™гБ©гБЃгВ®гГ≥гГЖгВ£гГЖгВ£гВТи§ЗжХ∞гВѓгГ©гВєгБЄеИЖй°ЮгБЧгБЊгБЩ
  • гГЗгГЉгВњгГђгВ≥гГЉгГЙеЖЕгБЃжђ†жРНжХ∞еА§е±ЮжАІгБЃдЇИжЄђгБКгВИгБ≥и£ЬеЃМгВТеЃЯзПЊгБЧгБЊгБЩ

гБУгБЃгВµгГЉгГУгВєгВТжіїзФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБдї•дЄЛгБЃгВИгБЖгБ™еКєжЮЬгБМжЬЯеЊЕгБІгБНгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • гГЗгГЉгВњзЃ°зРЖгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБЃиЗ™еЛХеМЦгГїйЂШйАЯеМЦ
  • гГЗгГЉгВњгГ°гГ≥гГЖгГКгГ≥гВєжЩВгБЃгВ®гГ©гГЉгБКгВИгБ≥жЙЛдљЬж•≠гБЃеЙКжЄЫ
  • гГЗгГЉгВњгБЃдЄАи≤ЂжАІгБКгВИгБ≥ж≠£зҐЇжАІгБЃеРСдЄК

 

Business Entity Recognition гБѓгАБйЭЮжІЛйА†еМЦгГЖгВ≠гВєгГИеЖЕгБЂеРЂгБЊгВМгВЛгАМгГУгВЄгГНгВєдЄКгБЃзЙєеЃЪи¶Бзі†пЉИгВ®гГ≥гГЖгВ£гГЖгВ£пЉЙгАНгВТиЗ™еЛХзЪДгБЂи¶ЛгБ§гБСеЗЇгБЧгБ¶еЉЈи™њи°®з§ЇгБЩгВЛгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБІгБЩгАВгБУгБУгБІгБДгБЖгВ®гГ≥гГЖгВ£гГЖгВ£гБ®гБѓгАБиЂЛж±ВжЫЄзХ™еПЈгГїй°ІеЃҐеРНгГїи£љеУБгВ≥гГЉгГЙгГїеПЦеЉХеЕИдЉБж•≠еРНгБ™гБ©гАБж•≠еЛЩгБІй†їеЗЇгБЩгВЛгАМи≠ШеИ•еПѓиГљгБ™еѓЊи±°гАНгВТжМЗгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВгБЯгБ®гБИгБ∞гАБиЂЛж±ВжЫЄгБЂйЦҐгБЩгВЛеХПгБДеРИгВПгБЫгГ°гГЉгГЂгБЛгВЙгАБиЂЛж±ВжЫЄзХ™еПЈгВДеПЦеЉХеЕИдЉБж•≠еРНгБ™гБ©гВТжКљеЗЇгБЧгАБиЂЛж±ВгГїжФѓжЙХгБДзКґж≥БгБЂйЦҐгБЩгВЛзє∞гВКињФгБЧзЩЇзФЯгБЩгВЛеХПгБДеРИгВПгБЫеѓЊењЬгВТиЗ™еЛХеМЦгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩгАВ

 

Machine TranslationпЉИж©ЯжҐ∞зњїи®≥пЉЙ

SAPгБѓдЄЦзХМеРДеЬ∞гБІгАБгБХгБЊгБЦгБЊгБ™и®Аи™ЮгБІеИ©зФ®гБХгВМгБ¶гБКгВКгАБгБїгБЉеЕ®гБ¶гБЃи®Аи™ЮгБЂеѓЊењЬгБЩгВЛењЕи¶БгБМгБВгВКгБЊгБЩгАВгБЭгБЃгБЯгВБгАБењЕи¶БгБ™и®Аи™ЮгБЄгБЃгГЖгВ≠гВєгГИзњїи®≥гБѓйЗНи¶БгБ™и™≤й°МгБ®гБ™гВКгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгБЃзњїи®≥гГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгВТињЕйАЯеМЦгГїиЗ™еЛХеМЦгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЂгАБAI FoundationгБЃдЄАйГ®гБ®гБЧгБ¶гАМSAP Translation HubгАНгБМжПРдЊЫгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

SAP Translation Hub гБѓгАБи§ЗжХ∞гБЃи®Аи™ЮгБЄгБЃгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгГ≥гГДзњїи®≥гВТжФѓжПігБЩгВЛеЖНеИ©зФ®еПѓиГљгБ™гВµгГЉгГУгВєгБІгБЩгАВгБУгБЃгВµгГЉгГУгВєгВТеИ©зФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБгВљгГХгГИгВ¶гВІгВҐгГЖгВ≠гВєгГИпЉИдЊЛпЉЪUIгБЂи°®з§ЇгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгВЛгГЖгВ≠гВєгГИпЉЙгВДйЦҐйА£гГЙгВ≠гГ•гГ°гГ≥гГИгВТйЂШеУБи≥™гБЛгБ§йЂШз≤ЊеЇ¶гБІињЕйАЯгБЂзњїи®≥гБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВгБЊгБЯгАБSAPжЙњи™НжЄИгБњгБЃи®≥и™ЮгВДзФ®и™ЮйЫЖгАБгБЭгБЧгБ¶SAPеЫЇжЬЙгБЃгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгГ≥гГДгБЂзЙєеМЦгБЧгБ¶е≠¶зњТгБХгВМгБЯж©ЯжҐ∞зњїи®≥гВТжіїзФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБЂгВИгВКгАБзњїи®≥дљЬж•≠гВТеКєзОЗеМЦгБЧгАБеЫљйЪЫзЪДгБ™ж•≠еЛЩе±ХйЦЛгВТгВєгГ†гГЉгВЇгБЂгВµгГЭгГЉгГИгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ

Note: еЕ®гБ¶гБЃAIгВµгГЉгГУгВєгВДгБЭгБЃи©≥зі∞гБѓгАБSAP Discovery Center гБЊгБЯгБѓ Help Portal гБІ зҐЇи™НгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

SAPй°ІеЃҐгГїгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉгБѓ AI Business Services гВТгБ©гБЖжіїзФ®гБІгБНгВЛгБЛпЉЯ

й°ІеЃҐгВДгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉгБѓгАБжПРдЊЫгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгВЛAPIгВ®гГ≥гГЙгГЭгВ§гГ≥гГИгБЂжО•зґЪгБЧеСЉгБ≥еЗЇгБЩгБУгБ®гБІгАБSAPгБЃ AIгВµгГЉгГУгВєгВТиЗ™з§ЊгБЃж•≠еЛЩгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгВДгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЂзµ±еРИгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

дї•дЄЛгБѓгАБгБЭгБЃдЄАдЊЛгБІгБЩгАВ

WebгВµгВ§гГИи®™еХПиАЕгБЄгБЃ Personalized Recommendation

гБЯгБ®гБИгБ∞гАБPersonalized Recommendation гВТзФ®гБДгБ¶гАБWebгВµгВ§гГИи®™еХПиАЕгБЂеѓЊгБЧгБ¶йЦ≤и¶Іе±•ж≠ігВДгВҐгВ§гГЖгГ†жГЕе†±гБЂеЯЇгБ•гБПйЂШеЇ¶гБЂгГСгГЉгВљгГКгГ©гВ§гВЇгБХгВМгБЯгГђгВ≥гГ°гГ≥гГЗгГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВТжПРз§ЇгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

дї•дЄЛгБЃеЫ≥гБѓгАБгБУгБЃгГ¶гГЉгВєгВ±гГЉгВєгБЂгБКгБСгВЛгВҐгГЉгВ≠гГЖгВѓгГБгГ£гБЃеЕ®дљУеГПгВТз§ЇгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБУгБЃеЃЯзПЊгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБѓгАБдї•дЄЛгБЃгВєгГЖгГГгГЧгБІжІЛжИРгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

Note: еРДгВєгГЖгГГгГЧгБЃеЕЈдљУзЪДгБ™жЙЛй†ЖгБѓпљ§гБУгБ°гВЙгБЃгГБгГ•гГЉгГИгГ™гВҐгГЂгБІзҐЇи™НгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБЊгБЯгАБдЄКи®ШгБЃ Personalized Recommendation гБ®еРМжІШгБЃжЦєж≥ХгБІгАБдЄЛеЫ≥гБЃгВИгБЖгБЂдїЦгБЃAIгВµгГЉгГУгВєгВВжіїзФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩгАВ

 

SAP AIгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБЂйЦҐгБЩгВЛињљеК†гГБгГ•гГЉгГИгГ™гВҐгГЂ

Use Machine Learning to Extract Information from Business Documents and Enrich Data

Use Pre-Trained Machine Learning Models to Process Unstructured Text

Shape Machine Learning to Process Custom Business Documents

Use Machine Learning to Classify Data Records

Use Generative AI to Process Business Documents

 

2. Generative AI Management

AI FoundationгБЃ2гБ§зЫЃгБЃйЗНи¶БгБ™жІЛжИРи¶Бзі†гБМгАМGenerative AI ManagementгАНгБІгБЩгАВSAP BTPгБЂгБКгБДгБ¶пљ§гБУгБЃгВЂгГЖгВігГ™гБЂи©≤ељУгБЩгВЛгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБМгАМGenerative AI HubгАНгБІгБЩпљ° Generative AI HubгБЂгВИгБ£гБ¶пљ§жІШгАЕгБ™е§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгВТжіїзФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩпљ°

гБЭгВМгБІгБѓгАБGenerative AI HubгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶и©≥гБЧгБПи¶ЛгБ¶гБДгБНгБЊгБЧгВЗгБЖгАВ

 

Generative AI HubгБ®гБѓдљХгБЛпЉЯ

Generative AI HubгБѓгАБSAP BTPдЄКгБІGenerative AIгБЃж©ЯиГљгВТжМБгБ§гВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥йЦЛзЩЇгВТињЕйАЯгБЂеЃЯзПЊгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЃгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБІгБЩгАВгБУгБЃгВµгГЉгГУгВєгВТжіїзФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБйЦЛзЩЇиАЕгБѓ Azure OpenAI гБМжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛ GPT-4 гВДгВ™гГЉгГЧгГ≥гВљгГЉгВєгБЃ Falcon 40B гБ™гБ©гАБи§ЗжХ∞гБЃгГЧгГ≠гГРгВ§гГАгБМжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБЂгБЩгБРгБЂгВҐгВѓгВїгВєгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

Generative AI HubгБѓгАБSAP AI CoreзµМзФ±гБІгБЃгГЧгГ≠гВ∞гГ©гГ†гБЂгВИгВЛжУНдљЬ еПКгБ≥ SAP AI LaunchpadеЖЕгБЃгГЧгГђгВ§гВ∞гГ©гВ¶гГ≥гГЙгВТеИ©зФ®гБЧгБЯжУНдљЬ гБЂгВИгБ£гБ¶ и§ЗжХ∞гБЃGenerative AIгВТжіїзФ®гБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБХгВЙгБЂGenerative AI HubгБѓгАБгГЧгГ≠гГ≥гГЧгГИгВ®гГ≥гВЄгГЛгВҐгГ™гГ≥гВ∞ пљ• зФЯжИРзµРжЮЬгБЃж§Ьи®ЉгБ™гБ©гБЃж©ЯиГљ гВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБЂгВИгВКгАБSAP BTPдЄКгБІгБЃGenerative AIгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЃйЦЛзЩЇгВТгАБгВИгВКеЃЙеЕ®гБІдњ°й†ЉжАІгБЃйЂШгБДељҐгБІеК†йАЯгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

дї•дЄЛгБЃеЫ≥гБѓгАБGenerative AI HubгВТзФ®гБДгБ¶и§ЗжХ∞гБЃе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБЂгГЧгГ≠гГ≥гГЧгГИгВТйАБдњ°гБЧгАБгБЭгБЃзФЯжИРзµРжЮЬгВТжѓФиЉГгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБжЬАйБ©гБ™гГҐгГЗгГЂгВТзЙєеЃЪгБЩгВЛдЄАдЊЛгВТз§ЇгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

Note: дЄКи®ШгБЃзФїеГПгБѓгАБгБУгБ°гВЙгБЃгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБЛгВЙеЉХзФ®гБЧгБЯгВВгБЃгБІгБЩгАВ

Note: Generative AI HubгБЃи©≥зі∞гБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶гБѓгАМPart9 - Generative AI HubгБЃж¶Ви¶БгАНгВТеПВзЕІгБЧгБ¶гБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

 

AI Workload Management

AI Workload ManagementгБѓгАБгВЉгГ≠гБЛгВЙжЦ∞гБЧгБДAIгГҐгГЗгГЂгВТжІЛзѓЙгБЧгБЯгБДгАБгБЊгБЯгБѓгГЗгГЉгВњжЇЦеВЩгГїгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЃгГИгГђгГЉгГЛгГ≥гВ∞гГїжО®иЂЦгБЃеЃЯи°МгГїзµРжЮЬгБЃеИЖжЮРгБ®гБДгБ£гБЯAIгГѓгГЉгВѓгГХгГ≠гГЉгВТеЃЯи°МгБЧгБЯгБДйЦЛзЩЇиАЕгВТеѓЊи±°гБ®гБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВгБЊгБЯгАБж®ЩжЇЦеМЦгБХгВМгВєгВ±гГЉгГ©гГЦгГЂгБЛгБ§гВѓгГ©гВ¶гГЙгГЧгГ≠гГРгВ§гГАгГЉгБЂдЊЭе≠ШгБЧгБ™гБДжЦєж≥ХгБІAIйЦҐйА£гБЃгВњгВєгВѓгВТйБЛзФ®гБЧгБЯгБДе†іеРИгБЂгВВйБ©гБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВAI Workload ManagementгБІгБѓгАБAIгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЃдљЬжИРгГїгГИгГђгГЉгГЛгГ≥гВ∞гБЛгВЙз≤ЊеЇ¶и©ХдЊ°гБКгВИгБ≥жО®иЂЦзФ®гБЂеЕђйЦЛгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЃењЕи¶БгБ™гГДгГЉгГЂгВТгБЩгБєгБ¶жПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ

AI Workload ManagementгБЃдЄїгБ™гВµгГЉгГУгВєгБѓгАБжђ°гБЃ2гБ§гБІгБЩпЉЪ

  • SAP AI Core
  • SAP AI Launchpad

 

SAP AI Core гБ®гБѓдљХгБЛпЉЯ

SAP AI CoreгБѓгАБSAP BTPдЄКгБІеИ©зФ®еПѓиГљгБ™гАБйЂШж©ЯиГљгБ™AIгГ©гГ≥гВњгВ§гГ†гВТжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБІгБЩгАВSAP AI CoreгВТзФ®гБДгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБAIгГҐгГЗгГЂгВТеКєзОЗиЙѓгБПе§Іи¶Пж®°гБЂе≠¶зњТгБЧгБ™гБМгВЙгАБгГЧгГ©гВ§гГРгВЈгГЉгВДгВ≥гГ≥гГЧгГ©гВ§гВҐгГ≥гВєгБЃзҐЇдњЭгВТеЃЯзПЊгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ

SAP AI CoreгБЃдЄїгБ™еИ©зВєгБЃдЄАгБ§гБѓгАБеєЕеЇГгБДгГЧгГ©гГ≥гБЛгВЙGPUгВДCPUгВ≥гВҐгАБгГ°гГҐгГ™гБ™гБ©гБЃеЯЇзЫ§гБ®гБ™гВЛгВ§гГ≥гГХгГ©гГ™гВљгГЉгВєгВТйБЄжКЮгБІгБНгВЛзВєгБІгБЩгАВгБЊгБЯгАБиЗ™еЛХгВєгВ±гГЉгГ™гГ≥гВ∞ж©ЯиГљгВВеВЩгВПгБ£гБ¶гБКгВКгАБжЯФиїЯгБ™гГ™гВљгГЉгВєзЃ°зРЖгБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩгАВSAP AI CoreгБІеИ©зФ®еПѓиГљгБ™гБХгБЊгБЦгБЊгБ™гГ™гВљгГЉгВєгГЧгГ©гГ≥гБѓгАБгБУгБ°гВЙгБЛгВЙгБФзҐЇи™НгБДгБЯгБ†гБСгБЊгБЩгАВ

Note: SAP AI CoreгБЃи©≥зі∞гБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶гБѓгАМPart5 - SAP AI CoreгБЃж¶Ви¶БгАНгВТеПВзЕІгБЧгБ¶гБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

 

SAP AI Launchpad гБ®гБѓдљХгБЛпЉЯ

SAP AI Launchpad гБѓгАБSAP BTPдЄКгБІеИ©зФ®еПѓиГљгБ™гАБи§ЗжХ∞гБЃAIгГ©гГ≥гВњгВ§гГ†пЉИSAP AI Core гВТеРЂгВАпЉЙгБЂгБЊгБЯгБМгБ£гБ¶гАБAIгГѓгГЉгВѓгГХгГ≠гГЉгВДAIгГҐгГЗгГЂгВТзЃ°зРЖгГїеЃЯи°МгВТжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБІгБЩгАВ

гБЊгБЯгАБGenerative AI Hub гВТйАЪгБШгБ¶гАБгВЄгВІгГНгГђгГЉгГЖгВ£гГЦAIгБЃж©ЯиГљгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВгБХгВЙгБЂгАБзЫіжДЯзЪДгБ™гГ¶гГЉгВґгГЉгВ§гГ≥гВњгГЉгГХгВІгГЉгВєгВТйАЪгБШгБ¶гАБAIгВЈгГКгГ™гВ™гБЂгБКгБСгВЛгГ©гВ§гГХгВµгВ§гВѓгГЂзЃ°зРЖгВТдЄАеЕГеМЦгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБЂгВИгВКгАБгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЃжАІиГљжМЗж®ЩгВТзґЩзґЪзЪДгБЂзЫ£и¶ЦгБЧгАБењЕи¶БгБЂењЬгБШгБ¶еЖНе≠¶зњТгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩгАВ

Note: SAP AI Launchpad гБЃи©≥зі∞гБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶гБѓгАМPart6 - SAP AI LaunchpadгБЃж¶Ви¶БгАНгВТеПВзЕІгБЧгБ¶гБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

 

SAP AI CoreгБКгВИгБ≥SAP AI LaunchpadгБЂйЦҐгБЩгВЛйЗНи¶БгБ™гГЭгВ§гГ≥гГИ

  • SAP AI Core гБѓ SAP AI Launchpad гБ®гВЈгГЉгГ†гГђгВєгБЂйА£жРЇгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩ
  • AIгГѓгГЉгВѓгГХгГ≠гГЉзЃ°зРЖгГїгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгГїгВњгВєгВѓгВТеЃєжШУгБЂгБЩгВЛдљњгБДгВДгБЩгБДгВ§гГ≥гВњгГЉгГХгВІгГЉгВєгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩ
  • Postman гВД PythonгВєгВѓгГ™гГЧгГИ гВТдљњзФ®гБЧгБ¶жУНдљЬгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гВВеПѓиГљгБІгБЩ
  • SAP BTPдЄКгБІгГЮгГЂгГБгГЖгГКгГ≥гГИгБ™SaaSгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБ®гБЧгБ¶еЛХдљЬгБЧгБЊгБЩ
  • пЉИSAP AI CoreгВ§гГ≥гВєгВњгГ≥гВєгВТеРЂгВАпЉЙи§ЗжХ∞гБЃ AIгГ©гГ≥гВњгВ§гГ†гВТдЄАеЕГзЪДгБЂзЃ°зРЖгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЃгГЧгГ©гГГгГИгГХгВ©гГЉгГ†гБ®гБЧгБ¶еИ©зФ®гБІгБНгБЊгБЩ

 

гГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгБ®гВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИ

SAPгВЈгВєгГЖгГ†гБМдїЦгБЃAIгГЧгГ≠гГРгВ§гГАгБ®е§ІгБНгБПзХ∞гБ™гВЛгБЃгБѓгАБй°ІеЃҐгБЃиЖ®е§ІгБ™гГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгВТдњЭжМБгБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛзВєгБІгБЩгАВгБУгВМгВЙгБЃгГЗгГЉгВњгБУгБЭгБМгАБAIгГҐгГЗгГЂгВТзФ®гБДгБ¶еЃЯйЪЫгБЃгГУгВЄгГНгВєи™≤й°МгВТиІ£ж±ЇгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЃйНµгБ®гБ™гВКгБЊгБЩгАВ

SAP HANA Cloud гВД SAP Datasphere гБ™гБ©гБЃгВљгГ™гГ•гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВТйАЪгБШгБ¶гАБSAPгБѓгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгВДгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгВТAIгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЂжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВзЙєгБЂ SAP HANA Cloud vector engine гБѓгАБGenerative AI гБЃгГ¶гГЉгВєгВ±гГЉгВєгБЂеѓЊењЬгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЂзЙєеИ•гБЂи®≠и®ИгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

SAP HANA Cloud vector engine гБ®гБѓдљХгБЛпЉЯ

SAP HANA Cloud vector engine гБѓгАБSAP BTPдЄКгБІжПРдЊЫгБХгВМгВЛ SAP HANA Cloud гБЂжЦ∞гБЯгБЂињљеК†гБХгВМгБЯж©ЯиГљгБІгБЩгАВSAP HANA Cloud vector engine гВТзФ®гБДгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБзЛђиЗ™гБЃгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгВТжіїзФ®гБЧгБЯAIгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЃеЯЇзЫ§гВТжІЛзѓЙгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

SAP HANA Cloud vector engine гБѓгАБгГЩгВѓгВњгГЉгГЗгГЉгВњгБЃеКєзОЗзЪДгБ™ж†ЉзіНгГїеЗ¶зРЖгВТгВµгГЭгГЉгГИгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВгБ§гБЊгВКгАБгГИгГ©гГ≥гВґгВѓгВЈгГІгГ≥гГЗгГЉгВњгАБз©ЇйЦУжГЕе†±гАБгВ∞гГ©гГХгГЗгГЉгВњгАБJSONгГЗгГЉгВњгБ™гБ©гАБе§Ъељ©гБ™гГЗгГЉгВњгВњгВ§гГЧгВТеРМгБШSQLзТ∞еҐГгБІгГЩгВѓгГИгГЂгГЗгГЉгВњгБ®зµДгБњеРИгВПгБЫгАБзµРеРИгГїгГХгВ£гГЂгВњгГ™гГ≥гВ∞гГїйБЄжКЮгБ®гБДгБ£гБЯжУНдљЬгБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩпљ°

дї•дЄЛгБѓгАБSAP HANA Cloud vector engine гБМгВВгБЯгВЙгБЩдЄїгБ™еИ©зВєгБІгБЩпЉЪ

  • гГЩгВѓгГИгГЂгВДгБЭгБЃдїЦгБЃгВ®гГ≥гВњгГЉгГЧгГ©гВ§гВЇгГЗгГЉгВњгВТеРМдЄАгГЗгГЉгВњгГЩгГЉгВєеЖЕгБЂж†ЉзіНгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБгГЗгГЉгВњгВҐгГЉгВ≠гГЖгВѓгГБгГ£гГїзЃ°зРЖгГїгВїгВ≠гГ•гГ™гГЖгВ£гВТз∞°зі†еМЦгБЧгБЊгБЩ
  • гГЩгВѓгВњгГЉгГЗгГЉгВњгВТеРЂгВАгБВгВЙгВЖгВЛгГЗгГЉгВњгВњгВ§гГЧгБЂеѓЊгБЧгБ¶SQLгВТзФ®гБДгБЯжУНдљЬгБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩ
  • з©ЇйЦУгГЗгГЉгВњгГїгВ∞гГ©гГХгГЗгГЉгВњгГїJSONгГЗгГЉгВњгАБгВЂгВєгВњгГ†SQLScriptгВТгГЩгГЉгВєгБЂгБЧгБЯгВѓгВ®гГ™гБ®гГЩгВѓгГИгГЂгВѓгВ®гГ™гВТзµДгБњеРИгВПгБЫгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБжЦ∞гБЯгБ™гГЗгГЉгВњгВ§гГ≥гВµгВ§гГИгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩ
  • SAP HANA CloudгВѓгГ©гВ§гВҐгГ≥гГИпЉИPythonеѓЊењЬпЉЙгАБPython Machine Learning Client for SAP HANAпЉИhana-mlпЉЙгАБCAPпЉИCloud Application ProgrammingпЉЙгВТзФ®гБДгБЯгГЩгВѓгВњгГЉгГЗгГЉгВњгБЂеЯЇгБ•гБПгВљгГ™гГ•гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВТжІЛзѓЙгБІгБНгБЊгБЩ

Note: SAP HANA Cloud vector engineгБЃи©≥зі∞гБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶гБѓгАМPart10 - SAP HANA Cloud vector engineгАНгВТеПВзЕІгБЧгБ¶гБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

 

SAP HANA CloudгБѓгАБдЇИжЄђеИЖжЮРзФ®гБЃгГ©гВ§гГЦгГ©гГ™гБІгБВгВЛPredictive Analysis LibraryпЉИPALпЉЙеПКгБ≥ иЗ™еЛХдЇИжЄђзФ®гБЃгГ©гВ§гГЦгГ©гГ™гБІгБВгВЛ Automated Predictive LibraryпЉИAPLпЉЙгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгВМгВЙгБЃгГ©гВ§гГЦгГ©гГ™гВТзФ®гБДгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгБЂзЫіжО•йБ©зФ®еПѓиГљгБ™еИЖй°ЮгГїеЫЮеЄ∞гГїжЩВз≥їеИЧдЇИжЄђгБ™гБ©гБЃгВЈгГКгГ™гВ™гВТеЃЯи£ЕгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБЊгБЯгАБSAPгБѓгГЗгГЉгВњзЃ°зРЖеРСгБСгБЂSAP DatasphereгБ®гБДгБЖгВљгГ™гГ•гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВВжПРдЊЫгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВSAP Datasphere гБѓгГЗгГЉгВњзµ±еРИгГїгГЗгГЉгВњеУБи≥™зЃ°зРЖгГїгВѓгГђгГ≥гВЄгГ≥гВ∞пЉИжђ†жРНеА§гВДйЗНи§ЗеА§гБЃи£Ьж≠£гБ™гБ©пЉЙгГїгГЗгГЉгВњгВђгГРгГКгГ≥гВєгГїгВҐгГКгГ™гГЖгВ£гВѓгВєпЉИгГИгГђгГ≥гГЙж§ЬеЗЇгАБйБОеОїгГЗгГЉгВњеИЖжЮРгАБе∞ЖжЭ•дЇИжЄђгБ™гБ©пЉЙгГїиЗ™зДґи®Аи™ЮеЗ¶зРЖгБ®гБДгБ£гБЯжІШгАЕгБ™ељҐгБІAIгВТжіїзФ®гБЧгАБгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгВТжЬАе§ІйЩРгБЂзФЯгБЛгБЫгВЛзТ∞еҐГгВТжХігБИгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

Foundation Model

AI Foundation гБЃжЮ†зµДгБњгБЃгВВгБ®гАБSAP BTPгБѓгБХгБЊгБЦгБЊгБ™Generative AI гВТзФ®гБДгБЯгГ¶гГЉгВєгВ±гГЉгВєгВТеЃЯзПЊгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЃеєЕеЇГгБД Foundation Model гВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВSAP гБѓ GoogleгГїMetaгГїIBMгГїNvidia гБ™гБ©гБЃдЄїи¶БгБ™ж±ОзФ®AIгГЩгГ≥гГАгГЉгВДе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгГЧгГ≠гГРгВ§гГАгГЉгБ®жПРжРЇгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБй°ІеЃҐгБЂгВЈгГЉгГ†гГђгВєгБ™дљУй®УгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБУгВМгВЙгБЃгГХгВ°гВ¶гГ≥гГЗгГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гГҐгГЗгГЂгБЂгВИгВКгАБSAPгБЃй°ІеЃҐгБѓжА•йАЯгБЂйА≤еМЦгБЩгВЛгВ§гГОгГЩгГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЂеѓЊењЬгБЧгБ§гБ§гАБиЗ™иЇЂгБЃгВЈгГКгГ™гВ™гБЂжЬАйБ©гБ™гГҐгГЗгГЂгВТжЯФиїЯгБЂйБЄжКЮгБІгБНгВЛгВИгБЖгБЂгБ™гВКгБЊгБЩгАВ

Foundation Model гБ®гБѓдљХгБЛпЉЯ

Foundation ModelгБѓгАБе§Ъз®Ѓе§ЪжІШгБ™ељҐеЉПгБЃгГЗгГЉгВњгВТе§ІйЗПгБЂе≠¶зњТгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІзЙєеЃЪгБЃеИЖйЗОгБЂгБКгБСгВЛдїїжДПгБЃгВњгВєгВѓгБЂеѓЊењЬгБІгБНгВЛгАБжХЩеЄЂгБ™гБЧе≠¶зњТгВТзФ®гБДгБ¶е≠¶зњТгБХгВМгБЯжЈ±е±§е≠¶зњТгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЃгБУгБ®гБІгБЩгАВгГЖгВ≠гВєгГИзФЯжИРгГїзФїеГПзФЯжИРгБ™гБ©еєЕеЇГгБДгВњгВєгВѓгБЂйБ©ењЬгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

Foundation ModelгБѓгАБжІШгАЕгБ™гГ¶гГЉгВєгВ±гГЉгВєгВТгВµгГЭгГЉгГИгБЩгВЛж±ОзФ®зЪДгБ™жКАи°УгБ®и¶ЛгБ™гБХгВМгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгВМгВЙгБЃгГҐгГЗгГЂгБѓгАБеЉЈеКЫгБ™гГПгГЉгГЙгВ¶гВІгВҐгБ®йЂШеЇ¶гБ™жКАи°УгВТдљњзФ®гБЧгБ¶е§Іи¶Пж®°гБ™гГЗгГЉгВњгВїгГГгГИгБІдЇЛеЙНгБЂе≠¶зњТгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгВЛгБЯгВБгАБзЙєеЃЪгБЃгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБФгБ®гБЂжЦ∞гБЧгБДгГҐгГЗгГЂгВТгГИгГђгГЉгГЛгГ≥гВ∞гБЩгВЛењЕи¶БгБМзД°гБПгАБжЩВйЦУгБ®и®ИзЃЧгВ≥гВєгГИгВТеЙКжЄЫгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

Note: и©≥зі∞гБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶гБѓгАМWhat is Foundation Model?гАНгВТгБФеПВзЕІгБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

 

SAP Foundation Models вАУ дЉБж•≠AIгВТеЖНеЃЪзЊ©гБЩгВЛSAPзЛђиЗ™гБЃFoundation Model

гБЊгБЪгБѓгАБгБ™гБЬй°ІеЃҐгБМSAPзЛђиЗ™гБЃ Foundation Model гВТењЕи¶БгБ®гБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛгБЃгБЛгВТиАГгБИгБ¶гБњгБЊгБЧгВЗгБЖгАВ

ж±ОзФ®зЪДгБ™ Foundation Model гБ†гБСгБІгБѓгАБSAPгБЃй°ІеЃҐгБЂеНБеИЖгБІгБ™гБДзРЖзФ±

иЖ®е§ІгБ™гГЗгГЉгВњгБІдЇЛеЙНе≠¶зњТгБХгВМгБЯж±ОзФ®зЪДгБ™ Foundation Model гБѓжХ∞е§ЪгБПе≠ШеЬ®гБЧгБЊгБЩгАВAI FoundationгБІгБѓгАБGPT-4гАБLlama2гАБFalcon-40bгАБClaude2гБ™гБ©гАБгБУгБЖгБЧгБЯе§ЪжІШгБ™е§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБЄеЃєжШУгБЂгВҐгВѓгВїгВєгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгВМгВЙгБЃгГҐгГЗгГЂгБѓзі†жЩігВЙгБЧгБДжАІиГљгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩгБМгАБгБДгБПгБ§гБЛгБЃи™≤й°МгВВе≠ШеЬ®гБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ

е§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБѓеП§гБДгГИгГђгГЉгГЛгГ≥гВ∞гГЗгГЉгВњгБЂдЊЭе≠ШгБЩгВЛеПѓиГљжАІ

е§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгВТйЭЮеЄЄгБЂеД™зІАгБ™еРМеГЪгБЂдЊЛгБИгБ¶гБњгБЊгБЩгАВгБЭгБЃеРМеГЪгБѓе§ЪгБПгБЃгБУгБ®гВТзЯ•гБ£гБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгБМгАБжШ®еєідЄЦзХМгБІдљХгБМиµЈгБНгБЯгБЛзЯ•гВЙгБЪгАБгБВгБ™гБЯгБЃдЉЪз§ЊеЫЇжЬЙгБЃеЖЕйГ®жЙЛзґЪгБНгВДжЦєйЗЭгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶гВВзРЖиІ£гБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЫгВУгАВгБУгВМгБѓгАБе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБМе≠¶зњТжЩВзВєгБЊгБІгБЃгГЗгГЉгВњгВТеЯЇгБЂе≠¶зњТгБЧгБ¶гБКгВКгАБгБЭгВМдї•йЩНгБЃжЦ∞гБЧгБДжГЕе†±гВТжМБгБ£гБ¶гБДгБ™гБДгБЯгВБгБІгБЩгАВ

еАЛгАЕгБЃдЉБж•≠еЫЇжЬЙгБЃгГЗгГЉгВњгВДгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБЃгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгБМжђ†е¶В

гАМеЕИйА±гАБгВПгБМз§ЊгБІжЬАгВВйЗНи¶БгБ™гВµгГЧгГ©гВ§гГ§гГЉгБЛгВЙеПЧгБСгБЯгВ™гГХгВ°гГЉгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶гБ©гБЖжАЭгБЖпЉЯгАНгБ®е§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБЂе∞ЛгБ≠гБ¶гВВж≠£зҐЇгБ™еЫЮз≠ФгВТињФгБЫгБ™гБДе†іеРИгБМгБВгВКгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБѓгАБе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLMпЉЙгБМж±ОзФ®зЪДгБ™зЯ•и≠ШгБІжІЛжИРгБХгВМгБ¶гБКгВКгАБзЙєеЃЪгБЃдЉБж•≠гГЗгГЉгВњгВДгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБЃгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶е≠¶зњТгБЧгБ¶гБДгБ™гБДгБЯгВБгБІгБЩгАВ

гБУгВМгВЙгБЃеХПй°МгБѓгАБйБ©еИЗгБ™гГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгВДгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгВТи£Ьиґ≥гБЩгВМгБ∞иІ£жґИгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгБУгБІзЩїе†ігБЩгВЛгБЃгБМ SAP Foundation Model гБІгБЩгАВSAPгБѓгАБSAPеЫЇжЬЙгБЃгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгБ®гВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгВТLLMгБЂеПЦгВКеЕ•гВМгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБй°ІеЃҐгБЂгБ®гБ£гБ¶жЬАйБ©гБ™гГҐгГЗгГЂгВТзФЯгБњеЗЇгБЭгБЖгБ®гБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

SAP Foundation Model гБ®гБѓдљХгБЛпЉЯ

SAPгБѓгАБж±ОзФ®зЪДгБ™ Foundation Model гБ®жЈ±гБДгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєзЯ•и¶ЛгБКгВИгБ≥гГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгВТзµДгБњеРИгВПгБЫгАБзЛђиЗ™гБЃ Foundation Model гВТи®≠и®ИгБЧгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгБЃгГҐгГЗгГЂгБЂгВИгВКгАБй°ІеЃҐгБѓзЙєеЃЪгБЃгГУгВЄгГНгВєгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгБЂеН≥гБЧгБЯеЫЮз≠ФгВТеЊЧгВЙгВМгВЛгВИгБЖгБЂгБ™гВКгБЊгБЩгАВ

SAP Foundation Model гБѓгАБи≤°еЛЩгГїеЦґж•≠гГїгВµгГЧгГ©гВ§гГБгВІгГЉгГ≥гБ™гБ©гАБгБВгВЙгВЖгВЛдЄїи¶БгБ™гГУгВЄгГНгВєй†ШеЯЯгБЂгБКгБДгБ¶йЭЮеЄЄгБЂжЬЙзФ®гБІгБЩгАВиЂЛж±ВжЫЄгБЃжФѓжЙХжЧ•гВТдЇИжЄђгБЧгБЯгВКгАБгВµгГЧгГ©гВ§гГ§гГЉгБЃзіНеУБеУБи≥™гВТи©ХдЊ°гБЧгБЯгВКгАБгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБЃеКєзОЗеМЦгВТжПРж°ИгБЧгБЯгВКгБ®гБДгБ£гБЯгАБж±ОзФ®зЪДгБ™ Foundation Model гБІгБѓеѓЊењЬгБМйЫ£гБЧгБДжЧ•гАЕгБЃгГУгВЄгГНгВєи™≤й°МгБЂгВВзЪДзҐЇгБЂењЬгБИгВЛгБУгБ®гБМеПѓиГљгБЂгБ™гВКгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБХгВЙгБЂгАБSAP Foundation Model гБѓгАБзЙєеЃЪгБЃгВЈгГКгГ™гВ™гБЂеРИгВПгБЫгБЯгГХгВ°гВ§гГ≥гГБгГ•гГЉгГЛгГ≥гВ∞(ињљеК†е≠¶зњТ)гВВеПѓиГљгБІгБВгВКгАБзµДгБњиЊЉгБњеЮЛAIеИЖйЗОгБІгБЃSAPгБЃгГ™гГЉгГАгГЉгВЈгГГгГЧгВТгВИгВКеЉЈеМЦгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВй°ІеЃҐгБѓи±КеѓМгБ™SAPгГЗгГЉгВњгБІе≠¶зњТгБХгВМгБЯйЂШжАІиГљгБ™гГҐгГЗгГЂгВТжіїзФ®гБЧгАБгВИгВКеД™гВМгБЯдЇИжЄђзµРжЮЬгВТеЊЧгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

гБ™гБКгАБSAP Foundation Model гБМеИ©зФ®еПѓиГљгБЂгБ™гВЛгБЃгВТеЊЕгБЯгБЪгАБдїКгБЩгБРгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгБЃгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгВТгВТжіїзФ®гБЧгБЯгБДе†іеРИгБѓгАБRetrieval-Augmented GenerationпЉИRAGпЉЙгБ®еСЉгБ∞гВМгВЛеИ•гБЃгВҐгГЧгГ≠гГЉгГБгВТзФ®гБДгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгБЃгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгВТеН≥еЇІгБЂжіїзФ®гБЧгАБгВИгВКиЙѓгБДзµРжЮЬгВТеЊЧгВЛгБУгБ®гВВеПѓиГљгБІгБЩгАВ

Note: RAGгБЃи©≥зі∞гБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶гБѓгАМPart11 - RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) гГїSAP AI CoreгГїGenerative AI HubгГїHANA Cloud vector engine гВТзФ®гБДгБЯеЃЯйЪЫгБЃгГ¶гГЉгВєгВ±гГЉгВєгАНгВТеПВзЕІгБЧгБ¶гБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

 

WhatвАЩs Next?
