жЬђгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБЂи®ШиЉЙгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгВЛгБЩгБєгБ¶гБЃи¶ЛиІ£гБКгВИгБ≥жДПи¶ЛгБѓгАБзІБеАЛдЇЇгБЃгВВгБЃгБІгБВгВКгАБеАЛдЇЇзЪДгБ™зЂЛе†ігБІињ∞гБєгВЙгВМгБЯгВВгБЃгБІгБЩгАВSAPгБѓгАБжЬђгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБЂжО≤иЉЙгБХгВМгБЯеЖЕеЃєгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶дЄАеИЗгБЃи≤ђдїїгВТи≤†гБДгБЊгБЫгВУгБЃгБІгАБгБВгВЙгБЛгБШгВБгБФдЇЖжЙњгБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

entry.title: Linked analysis based on widgets having different data sources with no common dimension in SAC
entry.content:
In SAP Analytics Cloud, stories often contain widgets such as charts and tables, which can connect to different models. Linked analysis helps to create dynamic interactions between said widgets and can be performed on widgets living within the same model or even across different models that contain at least one common dimension SAP Analytics Cloud 

But what need to be done when Widgets are based on different data sources with no common dimension.

 In the blog, we are going to discuss to have Linked analysis based on widgets having different data sources with no common dimension in SAC Application using Scripting. @VengatG 

 LetвАЩs see how the application should behave on Page Refresh

  • Dropdown 1 should have Company Code based on what user is authorized to and Dropdown 1 need to show default Company Code
  • Dropdown 2 should show Division corresponding to Company Code Selected and should be dynamically change with Company Code selection
  • Dropdown 3 should have Year/month with default value asвАЭ01.2024вАЭ
  • Dashboard should be refreshed based on the Default Values selected in Dropdowns

Snapshot of Dashboard on Page Refresh

We will discuss with few more exciting dynamic functionalities in next section.

Models/Popup Required/Created

We have utilized multiple models in the document as below

  • Dimension Model
  • Fact Data Model
  • Company Model

Table and Model naming in SAC Application

Table_1 corresponds to Dimension Model

Table_2 corresponds to Fact Data Model

Table_3 corresponds to Company Mapping Model

Achieving Requirement on data Refresh Page

Below logic need to be written on вАЬonInitilizationвАЭ Function of the application page

Dropdown 1 should have Company Code based on what user is authorized to and Dropdown 1 need to show default Company Code

var resultSetCompany =  Table_3.getDataSource().getResultSet();

for(var c=0; c<resultSetCompany.length; c++)   {   

  if(  resultSetCompany[c]["Company_Code"].id !=="#" )

                             {   if(c===0){

                             var default_value_Company=resultSetCompany[c]["Company_Code"].id;   }

                                       Dropdown_1.addItem(resultSetCompany[c]["Company_Code"].id); }  }

//set default value for Company code

Dropdown_1.setSelectedKey(default_value_Company);

Dropdown 2 should show Division corresponding to Company Code Selected and should be dynamically change with Company Code selection

//Loading  Division to dropdown_2

var  resultSetDivision = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultSet();

// Get Company Code selected by user

var company = default_value_Company;

var division_value_bool=true;

//Remove all items in the Division dropdown

Dropdown_2.removeAllItems();

// Fill only Division relevant to selected Company

for ( d = 0; i< resultSetDivision.length; d++ ){

              if ( resultSetDivision[d]["Company_Code"].id === company){

                             if(division_value_bool){

                                           var default_division = resultSetDivision[d]["Division"].id;

                                           division_value_bool = false;      }

                                           Dropdown_2.addItem(resultSetDivision[d]["Division"].id);  }}

//set default value for Division

Dropdown_2.setSelectedKey(default_division);

Dropdown 3 should have Year/month with default value asвАЭ01.2024вАЭ

Below is the sample Logic but you can change the logic based on the format, you want to see period in Dropdown

var startDateDropdown = new Date(2023, 12); //Period from which the dropdown should start showing the period

var endDateDropdown = new Date();

while(startDateDropdown <= endDateDropdown){  

   var month = startDateDropdown.getMonth();

   var year = startDateDropdown.getFullYear();

   if ( month < 12 ) {  month =  month + 1;

   } else {  year = year + 1;

                month = 1;

                startDateDropdown.setFullYear(year);   }

startDateDropdown.setMonth(month);

 Dropdown_3.addItem(month.concat(".").concat(year.toString()));   }

//Set default value for Period

Dropdown_3.setSelectedKey('01.2024');

Dashboard should be refreshed based on the Default Values selected in Dropdowns

//Applying Filter on Fact table based on dropdown values selected

var Company_filter = Dropdown_1.getSelectedKey();

var Division_filter = Dropdown_2.getSelectedKey();

var Period_filter = Dropdown_3.getSelectedKey();

Appling filter of selected values from Dropdown to Fact Table

Table_2.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("CompanyCode",Company_filter);

Table_2.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Period",Division_filter);

Table_2.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("Period",Period_filter);

entry.title: How Generative AI is Transforming Supply Chain Planning with SAP Solutions
entry.content:
Understanding Generative AI

Before we dive into the specifics of SAP, letвАЩs talk about what generative AI actually is. Unlike traditional AI, which analyzes existing data to make predictions, generative AI creates new content. This could mean generating supply chain models, optimizing routes, or even proposing innovative solutions for logistical challenges.

Why is this important for supply chains? Because supply chains thrive on adaptability, and generative AI offers the tools to build new strategies when the unexpected happens. Think of it as a Swiss Army knifeвАФalways ready with a solution for any scenario.

The Role of AI in Modern Supply Chains

AI is no longer just a buzzword. ItвАЩs an operational necessity in todayвАЩs fast-paced world. From real-time demand forecasting to streamlining manufacturing, AI has transformed supply chains into intelligent ecosystems. But the real magic happens when generative AI steps in. ItвАЩs not just about analyzing data; itвАЩs about creating solutions that didnвАЩt exist before.

SAP Solutions Enhancing Supply Chain Efficiency

SAP, a leader in enterprise software, has gone all-in on AI to tackle supply chain inefficiencies. Their tools donвАЩt just stop at automating tasks; they drive real insight and innovation. Drawing from our experience, SAPвАЩs solutions work seamlessly to connect data points across an organization, ensuring end-to-end visibility.

Key Features of SAP's AI-Driven Supply Chain Solutions

  1. Integrated Data Platforms: SAP integrates data from multiple sources to provide a single, unified view.
  2. Predictive Analytics: Helps in anticipating demand, reducing waste, and planning better.
  3. Automation at Scale: Streamlines repetitive tasks, allowing teams to focus on strategic initiatives.

Generative AI Capabilities in SAP

SAP has added a layer of generative AI to its offerings, and the impact is remarkable. Our team discovered through using this product that it helps businesses:

  • Generate real-time supply chain models that respond to disruptions.
  • Create alternative scenarios for demand fluctuations.
  • Optimize supplier relationships by suggesting innovative contract terms.

Overview of Generative AI Applications

Generative AI isnвАЩt just about clever algorithms; itвАЩs about practical, impactful applications. For instance:

  • Dynamic Route Planning: Generative AI can map out the fastest and most cost-effective transportation routes.
  • Inventory Optimization: Avoid stockouts and overstock situations by generating precise inventory strategies.

Benefits for Supply Chain Planning

  • Faster Decision-Making: Real-time data insights enable instant responses.
  • Improved Forecast Accuracy: Advanced predictive models minimize risks.
  • Operational Efficiency: Save costs and reduce manual intervention.

Transforming Decision-Making Processes

Real-Time Data Utilization

With generative AI, supply chain managers donвАЩt just rely on historical data. Instead, real-time data becomes the cornerstone of decision-making. For example, SAPвАЩs AI solutions analyze live market conditions to adjust production schedules on the fly.

Predictive Analytics and Forecasting

After putting it to the test, we determined through our experiments that SAPвАЩs generative AI delivers unparalleled predictive accuracy. This allows businesses to anticipate disruptions like raw material shortages or shipping delays and plan accordingly.

Challenges and Considerations in Implementation

While generative AI offers exciting possibilities, itвАЩs not without challenges. Based on our firsthand experience, businesses must address the following:

Data Privacy and Ethical Use of AI

Ensuring compliance with data privacy regulations is paramount. Generative AI relies on large datasets, and improper handling could lead to ethical concerns.

Integrating AI with Existing Systems

Legacy systems often present integration hurdles. However, SAPвАЩs modular architecture makes it easier to adopt generative AI without overhauling your entire tech stack.

Case Study: Abto Software's Approach to AI in Supply Chains

Overview of Abto Software's Solutions

Abto Software, a prominent  .net software development company, has been at the forefront of AI integration in supply chains. They specialize in creating custom AI solutions that complement SAPвАЩs capabilities, focusing on predictive maintenance and real-time analytics.

Impact on Supply Chain Efficiency

Through our practical knowledge, weвАЩve seen AbtoвАЩs tools help businesses:

  • Reduce downtime by 30%.
  • Improve demand forecasting accuracy by 25%.
  • Cut transportation costs by optimizing delivery routes.

Future Trends in Supply Chain Management with AI

The Shift Towards Autonomous Supply Chains

Imagine a supply chain that runs itselfвАФwhere decisions are made autonomously. This is no longer a distant dream, thanks to generative AI. As per our expertise, the next wave of innovation will focus on achieving full autonomy.

Emerging Technologies Supporting AI Integration

Technologies like IoT (Internet of Things) and blockchain are pivotal for AI integration. For example, IoT devices provide real-time data, while blockchain ensures secure and transparent data sharing.

Comparative Analysis of SAP's AI Solutions

Feature

Traditional Methods

SAP AI Solutions

Decision-Making Speed

Slow

Real-time insights

Data Processing Capability

Limited

Advanced analytics

Predictive Accuracy

Moderate

High with machine learning

Conclusion: The Future of Supply Chain Planning with Generative AI

Generative AI is more than just a buzzword; itвАЩs the future of supply chain planning. By enabling real-time decision-making, improving accuracy, and fostering innovation, tools like SAPвАЩs AI solutions are transforming the supply chain landscape. Whether itвАЩs reducing costs or enhancing efficiency, the benefits are clear. For businesses ready to embrace this technology, the potential is limitless.

FAQs

  1. What is generative AI in supply chain planning? Generative AI creates new strategies and models to optimize supply chains, going beyond traditional predictive analytics.
  2. How does SAP leverage generative AI? SAP uses generative AI for real-time data analysis, predictive forecasting, and dynamic decision-making.
  3. What are the main benefits of generative AI in supply chains? It improves decision-making speed, enhances predictive accuracy, and reduces operational costs.
  4. What challenges come with implementing AI in supply chains? Key challenges include data privacy concerns, system integration, and the need for large datasets.
  5. How does Abto Software support AI in supply chains? Abto Software provides custom solutions for predictive maintenance, real-time analytics, and more.
  6. WhatвАЩs the future of AI in supply chain management? The future lies in autonomous supply chains powered by generative AI and supported by IoT and blockchain.
  7. Why should businesses consider SAP for supply chain AI? SAP offers robust, scalable, and innovative AI solutions that integrate seamlessly with existing systems.

entry.title: Digital Transformation. View of an Enterprise Architect - Artificial Intelligence (AI)
entry.content:
#GenerativeAI

 

Introduction: AI in SAP Enterprise Architecture - the rise

AI is playing an increasingly important role in the business world. Companies need to make their processes more efficient and cope with enormous amounts of data (Singla et al., 2024). Many SAP customers are therefore asking themselves which AI technology should best be used when in order to achieve the greatest possible benefit.

SAP offers a variety of AI tools and solutions to help companies optimize their business processes. These include SAP Joule, Embedded AI and the SAP AI Foundation. Another important element is SAP Datasphere, which serves as a central data consolidation layer and enables the integration of SAP and non-SAP data sources. In this blog, we will show you how you can use these technologies in a targeted manner to implement a future-proof and innovative SAP Enterprise Architecture.

 

 

 

 

What is SAP Enterprise Architecture in the context of AI?

The task of an SAP Enterprise Architect is to align a company's IT architecture in such a way that it optimally supports the strategic business objectives (Scarbrough et al., 2020; LeanIX, n.d.). This includes integrating new technologies such as AI to improve processes and increase business value.

However, the implementation of AI presents companies with challenges: Data must be consolidated, AI models must be trained and integrated into existing systems. This is where the role of the SAP Enterprise Architect comes into play, who maintains an overview of the various AI technologies and plans and manages their use sensibly.

 

SAP Joule: The AI co-pilot for efficient business processes

SAP Joule is an AI-supported co-pilot that helps companies to automate business processes and make them more efficient. Joule was developed specifically for SAP applications and integrates seamlessly into existing SAP systems. With Joule, companies can optimize their workflows, save time and minimize human error.

Some of the most important areas of application for SAP Joule are
1. Navigation: Joule simplifies navigation within SAP systems. Instead of knowing complicated transaction codes or menu paths, users can give Joule instructions in natural language
2. Transaction: Joule can help with the execution of transactions. Users can give Joule instructions to complete specific tasks.
3. Information: Joule can retrieve information from various SAP systems and data sources and present it in an understandable form. Instead of creating complex reports or searching in tables, users can ask Joule questions
4. Analysis: Joule can help analyze data and provide insights. Users can ask Joule to analyze specific data or identify trends

 

Benefits of SAP Joule:
- Time savings through automation of routine tasks.
- Improved decision making through data-based insights.
- Seamless integration into existing SAP systems without costly customization.

Companies that use SAP Joule benefit from greater efficiency and can better focus their resources on strategic tasks.

 

Embedded AI: Integrated AI functions in SAP applications

Embedded AI refers to the AI functions integrated directly into SAP applications. These solutions are specifically designed to improve existing business processes without the need to develop separate AI models or implement additional platforms. Embedded AI is integrated into many SAP products and enables companies to make AI-supported decisions directly within their daily processes (SAP, 2024).

Examples of embedded AI in the SAP landscape:
1. SAP S/4HANA: AI-supported financial planning and analysis, automatic variance detection in reports.
2. SAP SuccessFactors: Intelligent recommendations for applicant profiles and personnel development.
3. SAP Ariba: Optimized supplier evaluations and automated price analyses.


Advantages of embedded AI:
- Fast implementation: No separate AI infrastructure required.
- Process optimization: AI supports users directly in operational business.
- High user-friendliness: AI functions are integrated into familiar user interfaces.

With embedded AI, companies can gradually and pragmatically improve their business processes without having to make major changes to their IT landscape.

 

SAP AI Foundation: Scalable AI solutions for individual requirements

In contrast to the standardized AI functions of Embedded AI, the SAP AI Foundation enables companies to develop their own, highly specialized AI solutions and integrate them seamlessly into their existing SAP environment. These customized models can be tailored precisely to the individual requirements of each company.

Core functions of the SAP AI Foundation:
1. Machine Learning Operations (MLOps): Tools for developing, deploying and monitoring AI models.
2. Integration into SAP environments: Support for connecting to various SAP systems such as SAP S/4HANA.
3. Scalability: support for large data volumes and complex models that can be executed in the cloud or on-premise.

Application examples for the SAP AI Foundation:
- Predictive models for inventory management and demand planning.
- Customer-specific chatbots for customer service.
- Image and text analysis for the automation of quality controls.

 

 

The role of SAP Datasphere in data consolidation

In hybrid architectures where SAP and third-party solutions work together, SAP Datasphere forms a central layer to bundle data streams and make them available for AI models, for example by enabling the execution of Python scripts directly on the virtualized data, eliminating the need for data replication. This facilitates the development of AI applications based on large and diverse data sets

Functions of SAP Datasphere:
- Data integration: linking SAP and non-SAP data sources.
- Data virtualization: Access to data without physically moving it.
- Data quality and governance: Ensuring consistent and high-quality data for AI processes.

By connecting all relevant data sources and providing up-to-date, consistent and reliable data in real time, SAP Datasphere forms the basis for successful AI projects. This enables the optimal use of AI technologies from SAP and third-party providers.

Advantages of SAP Datasphere:
- Real-time data access for quick decisions.
- Integration of data islands for a uniform view of company data.
- Support for hybrid scenarios with cloud and on-premise data sources.

 

When which AI technology should be used?

Choosing the right AI technology depends heavily on a company's individual requirements and goals. Here is an overview of when SAP Joule, Embedded AI or the SAP AI Foundation are best suited:

1. SAP Joule:
When: For automating routine tasks and supporting users with intelligent recommendations.
Examples: Financial accounting, invoice processing, customer support.

2. Embedded AI:
When: For the optimization of standard processes within existing SAP applications.
applications.
Examples: HR management with SuccessFactors, inventory management with S/4HANA.

3. SAP AI Foundation:
When: For individual AI developments and complex models that have to fulfill specific
requirements must be met.
Examples: Predictive analytics, customer-specific chatbots, quality control with
image analysis.

Companies should first analyze their business processes and data landscape to decide which AI solution offers the greatest added value. A structured approach ensures that AI projects are implemented successfully.

 

Benefits for companies through intelligent SAP Enterprise Architecture

The integration of AI into SAP Enterprise Architecture offers companies numerous benefits, including

1. Increased efficiency: routine tasks can be automated, allowing employees to focus on strategic tasks.
2. Better decision-making: AI provides real-time data-based insights, leading to more informed decisions
3. Cost reduction: operational costs can be reduced through automation and optimized processes
4. Improved customer experience: AI-supported services and support improve interaction with customers.

AI is changing the rules of the game. In order to remain competitive, companies must adapt their business models to the new requirements and use AI in a targeted manner. Global economic growth through AI is estimated at up to 4.4 trillion US dollars per year (McKinsey&Company, 2023).

 

Tips for the successful use of AI in SAP architecture

Here are some recommendations for companies looking to integrate AI into their SAP landscape:

1. Start small, scale big: Start with pilot projects and expand gradually.
2. Develop a data strategy: Build a clear data strategy for the consolidation and use of data.
3. Choose the right AI solution: Decide whether SAP Joule, Embedded AI or the AI Foundation is best suited.
4. Involve employees: Conduct training and workshops for employees to create acceptance and understanding of AI.
5. Regularly monitor success: continuously monitor and optimize AI projects.

SAP workshops, such as the SAP Business AI JumpStart, show you how to identify the right AI solutions for your company and integrate them into your SAP landscape. Talk to your Account Executive to find out more.

 

Conclusion: AI as a driver for modern SAP enterprise architecture

AI is an indispensable component of a modern SAP enterprise architecture. With solutions such as SAP Joule, Embedded AI and the SAP AI Foundation, companies can automate their processes, make data-based decisions and increase their efficiency. SAP Datasphere provides the necessary basis for a consolidated and reliable database. Companies that invest in AI and a future-proof architecture now will secure long-term competitive advantages and be ready for the digital future.

 

FAQs

1. What is SAP Joule and how does it support companies?

SAP Joule is an AI-powered co-pilot that automates routine tasks and provides users with intelligent recommendations to make business processes more efficient.

2. What is the difference between Embedded AI and SAP AI Foundation?

Embedded AI offers predefined AI functions directly in SAP applications, while the SAP AI Foundation enables the development of individual, scalable AI models.

3. Why is SAP Datasphere important for AI applications?

SAP Datasphere consolidates data from various sources and makes it available for AI models in real time, which is essential for reliable analysis and automation.

4. What challenges are there when introducing AI in SAP systems?

Challenges include data quality, skills shortages, change management and compliance with data protection regulations.

5. How do I start an AI project in the SAP architecture?

Start with a pilot project, develop a clear data strategy, choose the right AI solution and involve employees at an early stage. Contact the manufacturers involved at an early stage to identify the right technology.

 

 

Common thread of this blog series:

  1. Introduction 
  2. Tasks of an Enterprise Architect 
  3. Success Factors for Digital Transformation Projects 
  4. Help of an Enterprise-Architecture-Framework 
  5. Steps and Tips 
  6. Artificial Intelligence Meets Enterprise Architecture

 

 

Bibliography:

LeanIX. (o.J.). SAP-Architekt. Retrieved from  https://www.leanix.net/de/wiki/tech-transformation/sap-architect

McKinsey & Company. (2023). The Economic Potential of Generative AI. Retrieved from https://www.mckinsey.com/featured-insights/mckinsey-explainers/what-is-generative-ai

SAP. (n.d.). AI Catalog вАУ SAP Discovery Center. Retrieved from https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog/

Scarbrough, M., et al. (2020). Enterprise Architecture with SAP: Planning, Management, and Transformation. Rheinwerk Verlag.

Singla, A., Sukharevsky, A., Yee, L. & Chui, M. (2024, 30. Mai). The state of AI in early 2024: Gen AI adoption spikes and starts to generate value. McKinsey & Company. https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-state-of-ai

entry.title: Orchestrate your Generative AI workflows through the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Authors: @amagnani |@jacobahtan |@thiago | @Trinidad

As we delve into the world of AI, it's essential to understand how orchestrating various components can enhance generative AI's efficiency and reliability. SAP's Generative AI Hub recently introduced a game-changing feature вАФ Orchestration. This feature is designed to do more than just provide model access; it's about creating robust AI workflows that seamlessly integrate with your business applications.

This blog post is part of the webinar series Augment Your SAP BTP Use Cases With AI Foundation on SAP BTP exploring the Generative AI capabilities of SAP AI Foundation and presents proof of concepts through various use cases.

 

Understanding the Power of Orchestration

In the realm of generative AI, especially within a business context, simply connecting to a language model is often inadequate. Business applications require structured, context-rich, and reliable outputs to deliver value. This is where SAPвАЩs Orchestration steps in, tying together various components like data pipelines, AI models, and prebuilt modules.

Orchestration is part of the Generative AI Hub and focuses on:

  • Connecting Components: Combines data pipelines, AI models, and modules like grounding and content filtering.
  • Reducing Maintenance: Simplifies the integration process, allowing businesses to focus on innovation.

Orchestration Key Features

Grounding

Grounding is crucial for contextual AI responses. It enriches the user's request with relevant business context, reducing the likelihood of AI hallucinations вАФ false or irrelevant information generated by AI models. By automating document grounding, businesses can integrate relevant document indexing, updated daily, without manual intervention.

Manual grounding techniques involve chunking and embedding steps, offering granular control over the data process (check our previous blog Embedding Business Context with the SAP HANA Cloud, Vector Engine for more details). However, SAPвАЩs Orchestration offers a streamlined alternative, connecting user queries to the business context through a retrieval layer. This involves:

  • Data Ingestion: Uploading relevant documents.
  • Preprocessing & Chunking: Breaking down documents into manageable parts.
  • Embedding & Similarity Search: Generating embeddings for efficient similarity search.

Prompt Templating

Prompt Templating enables the creation of templates containing placeholders that can be dynamically replaced with specific inputs or variables. This method facilitates the generation of diverse queries or instructions by substituting different values into the placeholders, thus simplifying the customization of prompts for various tasks or contexts. Prompt Templating produces the final query, which is then sent to the model.

Recently, a new Generative AI feature called Prompt Registry was introduced, providing a foundation for managing prompt templates throughout various AI scenarios. This feature supports the entire process, from design to runtime, and helps reduce the complexity associated with handling prompt templates. The Prompt Registry's functionalities can be accessed via APIs directly from the Generative AI Hub. Additionally, prompts stored in the Prompt Registry can be reused within the Orchestration module.

Data Masking

Despite the Generative AI Hub encrypting data by default, data masking is critical for additional privacy. It's about concealing sensitive information during processing and storage, as well as safeguarding against unauthorized access or bias.

The Data Masking capability offers a range of Masked Data Types you can select for masking, that are served by the SAP BTP Data Privacy Integration service APIs.

Two options are offered for Data Masking:

  • Anonymization: Replaces names with placeholders to protect personal data.
  • Pseudo-Anonymization: The orchestration service can reverse placeholders back to original text for final outputs.

Content Filtering

Content filtering safeguards content standards by offering both input and output filters. These filters ensure that AI responses align with your enterprise's guidelines, preventing inappropriate content.

  • Input Filtering: Checks incoming prompts for guideline alignment.
  • Output Filtering: Analyzes AI-generated responses to ensure they meet standards.

This dual-layer filtering enhances brand integrity, ensures regulatory compliance, and promotes user safety.

Harmonized API Management

The Orchestration feature also supports harmonized APIs, facilitating seamless model-switching by simply replacing the model name. This approach enhances developer efficiency by providing consistency across various generative AI models.

 

How to consume Orchestration capabilities

SAP AI Launchpad

The SAP AI Launchpad provides a low-code/no-code interface for configuring and accessing the orchestration features of the Generative AI Hub. It allows users to create and test orchestration workflows before integrating them into applications. The Launchpad includes a graphical interface as well as the option to generate workflows as JSON files.

Generative AI APIs and SDKs

For direct application integration, Generative AI orchestration capabilities are also accessible via APIs, eliminating the need for the SAP AI Launchpad.

SDKs

The Generative AI Hub offers a comprehensive suite of SDKs in various programming languages, designed to accelerate and simplify the development of custom AI solutions.

The SDKs provide an abstraction layer that reduces implementation complexity, automating tasks like OAuth token handling and deployment ID lookups, harmonizing LLM access, and facilitating orchestration capabilities such as content filtering and data masking. They support integration with frameworks like LangChain.
Three versions are available for you to choose:

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI (JavaScript)  
  • Python SAP generative AI hub SDK
  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI (Java)

     

вАЬAsk the CityвАЭ Use Case

We would like to illustrate the Orchestration capabilities and how to implement them, with a practical use case that we have called вАЬAsk the CityвАЭ.

Business Scenario

In a previous blog Embedding Business Context with the SAP HANA Cloud, Vector Engine, we already explored the "Ask the City" use case, where our primary persona, Emma, a city resident, requested answers to her inquiries through a chat interface, eliminating the need to manually sift through the city's website pages. In our previous blog, we harnessed the power of SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine alongside Generative AI to implement retrieval-augmented generation, commonly referred to as grounding capabilities. This approach allowed us to maintain granular control over each phase of the process. We meticulously managed document chunking, leveraged GenAI LLMs for embedding generation, stored the resulting vectors within SAP HANA Cloud, and selected the most appropriate similarity search algorithm.

In today's blog, we'll explore the implementing the "Ask the City" use case using the grounding capability within the orchestration layer of the Generative AI Hub. By employing the orchestration module, we can significantly simplify our code, as it eliminates the need to manually handle the various steps involved in retrieval-augmented generation, or grounding. Additionally, this approach allows us to leverage other capabilities provided by the orchestration module, such as data masking and content filtering. These features enable us to extend our use case coverage, enhancing its functionality and efficiency.

LetвАЩs demonstrate our prototype in action with a short demo video of the Ask The City chatbot.

Architecture

Let's now examine the architecture diagram of our prototype.

Citizens engage with the chatbot via a straightforward web chat embedded within the Sagenai City website.

This prototype

  • utilizes the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model
  • is deployed on the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Runtime
  • implements generative AI capabilities through the Orchestration service of the Generative AI Hub, managing documents stored on a Microsoft SharePoint site and connecting to the configured foundation model.

Implementation Deep Dive

LetвАЩs delve now into the detailed steps involved in implementing the orchestration capabilities of our prototype.

We've broken down this technical section into five steps for streamlined understanding:

  1. Generative AI Hub setup on our BTP subaccount
  2. SAP AI Core instance configuration to support grounding capabilities
  3. Orchestration configuration creation and deployment in SAP AI Core

For setting up our AI Core instance, we've utilized the AI Launchpad UI; however, you can alternatively use the corresponding APIs for configuration.

4. SharePoint repository setup, to store documents for grounding and integrating it with the grounding capabilities

вАГ5. "Ask the City" prototype application implementation.

Source Code

Our Ask the City use case prototype source code is available in Github: 04-ask-city-orchestration-service

It includes:

  • cap-chatbot: Full-stack SAP Cloud Application Program Model(CAP) TypeScript implementation, integrated with Orchestration Service through @sap-ai-sdk/orchestration.
  • python-notebooks: Notebook demonstrating how to interact with Orchestration Service to implement the AskTheCity Use Case with orchestration service through SAP Generative AI Hub SDK.

 

Additional Resources for you to explore

To wrap things up, we've compiled a selection of links to orchestration documents and educational sessions that you might find beneficial:

  • Learning Journey Generative AI Hub
    • Using generative-AI-hub-SDK to interact with Orchestration Services (includes a Notebook example)
  • SAP Help Center
    • Orchestration Service in SAP AI Launchpad
    • Orchestration Service in SAP AI Core
    • Prompt Registry
  • TechEd sessions
    • AI201 | Development with our generative AI hub, SDKs, and orchestration features
    • AI106 - Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and generative AI hub

Important Note: Conversion Rates for GenAI Tokens in Orchestration Modules supported regions for grounding

 

More sessions on this series

Don't miss out on our central blog post Augment Your SAP BTP Use Cases With AI Foundation on SAP BTP, where you'll find comprehensive details about the sessions in this series. This includes session recordings and related blog posts. Plus, you can easily register for upcoming sessions to continue expanding your knowledge and skills.

 

Conclusion and Takeaways

SAPвАЩs Orchestration feature within the Generative AI Hub allowing businesses to harness AI's potential effectively. By providing document grounding, prompt templating, data masking, content filtering, and harmonized APIs, it empowers enterprises to focus on innovation rather than integration challenges. Orchestration capabilities ensure your AI solutions are not only smarter but also safer and more reliable, paving the way for advanced AI-driven business outcomes.

entry.title: Agentic AI in SAP: Revolutionizing Business Processes
entry.content:
Introduction:

In today's rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses across industries are seeking innovative solutions to streamline operations and enhance decision-making. One such groundbreaking technology is Agentic AI, a form of artificial intelligence designed to act autonomously and drive business value. When integrated with SAP systems, Agentic AI can transform enterprise operations by automating complex processes, reducing human intervention, and improving accuracy.
This blog explores how Agentic AI is reshaping SAP business processes, enhancing efficiency, and delivering unprecedented value.

Understanding Agentic AI:

Agentic AI refers to intelligent systems that possess agencyвАФthe ability to perceive their environment, make decisions, and act independently to achieve specific goals. Unlike traditional automation, which follows predefined rules and scripts, Agentic AI leverages machine learning, natural language processing, and advanced analytics to:

  • Learn from historical data.
  • Predict outcomes.
  • Take proactive actions.

For enterprises running SAP, this capability opens doors to self-driven operations, significantly reducing manual intervention and operational errors.

Applications of Agentic AI in SAP

Agentic AI can revolutionize various aspects of SAP-driven enterprises by introducing a new level of automation and intelligence:

1. Intelligent Financial Operations:

  • Automating accounts payable and receivable processes.
  • Detecting anomalies in financial transactions.
  • Predicting cash flow and optimizing working capital.

2. Supply Chain Optimization:

  • Forecasting demand and automating inventory management.
  • Optimizing logistics and transportation routes in real-time.
  • Identifying supply chain risks and suggesting mitigations.

3. Human Resources Management:

  • Enhancing talent acquisition by screening resumes and predicting candidate success.
  • Automating employee engagement surveys and providing actionable insights.
  • Streamlining payroll and benefits administration.

4. Sales and Customer Relationship Management:

  • Predicting customer needs and personalizing sales strategies.
  • Automating lead generation and qualification processes.
  • Enhancing customer support with AI-powered chatbots.

5. Production and Manufacturing:

  • Monitoring equipment health and predicting maintenance needs.
  • Optimizing production schedules to minimize downtime.
  • Enhancing quality control with AI-driven defect detection.

Benefits of Agentic AI in SAP

Integrating Agentic AI into SAP systems offers numerous advantages:

  • Increased Efficiency: Automation of routine tasks allows employees to focus on strategic initiatives, enhancing overall productivity.
  • Improved Decision-Making: AI-driven insights enable informed decision-making, reducing reliance on intuition and guesswork.
  • Enhanced Accuracy: By minimizing human intervention, Agentic AI reduces the likelihood of errors in critical business processes.
  • Scalability: AI-driven processes can scale effortlessly, accommodating business growth without a proportional increase in operational costs.
  • Cost Savings: By optimizing resource utilization and reducing manual effort, businesses can achieve significant cost savings.

Challenges and Considerations

While the benefits of Agentic AI are compelling, businesses must address certain challenges to maximize its potential:

  • Data Quality: AI systems require high-quality data for accurate predictions. Organizations must invest in data cleansing and management.
  • Change Management: Adopting AI-driven processes may face resistance from employees. Effective change management strategies are crucial to ensure smooth adoption.
  • Integration Complexity: Seamless integration of Agentic AI with existing SAP systems requires careful planning and execution.
  • Ethical Concerns: Ensuring data privacy and addressing ethical implications of AI-driven decisions are vital.

The Future of Agentic AI in SAP

The adoption of Agentic AI in SAP is still in its early stages, but the potential is immense. Future advancements in AI technology will further enhance its capabilities, enabling:

  • Hyper-Automation: Extending automation beyond repetitive tasks to more complex decision-making processes.
  • Real-Time Analytics: Providing instant insights for faster and more accurate decision-making.
  • Personalized User Experiences: Tailoring interfaces and workflows to individual user preferences and behaviors.

As SAP systems continue to evolve, Agentic AI will play a pivotal role in driving innovation and ensuring businesses stay competitive in a digital-first world.

KTern.AI: Driving Innovation in SAP Transformations:

KTern.AI leverages Agentic AI to redefine SAP transformations, offering an AI-First Delivery approach that automates, optimizes, and accelerates digital transformation processes. HereвАЩs a snapshot of its core capabilities:

  • Automated Assessments: KTern.AI automates SAP landscape assessments, providing actionable insights to optimize custom codes, business processes, and system configurations. These data-driven insights enable proactive optimization, ensuring strategic and efficient transformations.
  • Risk-Based Testing: KTern.AI prioritizes testing efforts based on risk, focusing on critical business processes while ensuring comprehensive coverage. Its generative AI-driven test case recommendations enhance efficiency, accelerating project timelines without compromising quality.

Conclusion

Agentic AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses manage and optimize their operations. By enabling autonomous decision-making and action, it empowers organizations to achieve greater agility, efficiency, and resilience.
With KTern.AIвАЩs innovative capabilities, businesses can harness the full potential of Agentic AI within their SAP ecosystems, paving the way for a future defined by intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making.

JouleгБ®гБѓпЉЯ

дњ°й†ЉгБІгБНгВЛAIгБЃгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉгБМгБВгБ™гБЯгБЃгБЭгБ∞гБЂгБДгБ¶гАБиЗ™зДґгБ™и®Аи™ЮгБ®зФЯжИРиГљеКЫгВТйІЖдљњгБЧгАБSAPгБЃгВѓгГ©гВ¶гГЙгГЭгГЉгГИгГХгВ©гГ™гВ™еЕ®дљУгБІжЧ•гАЕгБЃж•≠еЛЩгВТгВµгГЭгГЉгГИгГїеРСдЄКгБХгБЫгБ¶гБПгВМгВЛзФЯжИРAI CopilotгАБгБЭгВМгБМJouleгБІгБЩгАВ
дї•дЄЛгБЂJouleгБЃдЄїи¶БгГЭгВ§гГ≥гГИгВТгБЊгБ®гВБгБЊгБЧгБЯгАВ

гБЭгВМгБЮгВМгБЃгГЭгВ§гГ≥гГИгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶и©≥гБЧгБПи¶ЛгБ¶гБДгБПеЙНгБЂгАБJouleгВТгВИгВКгВИгБПзРЖиІ£гБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгАБгБДгБПгБ§гБЛгБЃйЗНи¶БгБ™зФ®и™ЮгВТз∞°еНШгБЂжХізРЖгБЧгБ¶гБДгБНгБЊгБЧгВЗгБЖгАВ

 

Generative AI (зФЯжИРAI)гБ®гБѓпЉЯ

зФЯжИРAIгБѓгАБиКЄи°УдљЬеУБгАБйЯ≥ж•љгАБгГ™гВҐгГЂгБ™зФїеГПгБ™гБ©гАБжЦ∞гБЧгБДгВВгБЃгВТдљЬгВКеЗЇгБЩгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгВЛдЇЇеЈ•зЯ•иГљпЉИAIпЉЙгБЃдЄАз®ЃгБІгБЩгАВгБВгВЙгБЛгБШгВБдљХгВТдљЬгВЛгБЛгВТеЕЈдљУзЪДгБЂжМЗз§ЇгБХгВМгБ™гБПгБ¶гВВгАБжЦ∞гБЧгБДгВВгБЃгВТзФЯгБњеЗЇгБЫгВЛгБ®гБДгБЖзЙєеЊігБМгБВгВКгБЊгБЩгАВ
зФЯжИРAIгБѓгАБжЧҐе≠ШгБЃгГЗгГЉгВњгБЛгВЙгГСгВњгГЉгГ≥гВТе≠¶гБґгБУгБ®гБЂгВИгБ£гБ¶жЦ∞гБЧгБДгВ≥гГ≥гГЖгГ≥гГДгВТдљЬгВКеЗЇгБЩгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВгБЊгВЛгБІгВѓгГ™гВ®гВ§гГЖгВ£гГЦгБ™ж©ЯжҐ∞гБМгАБи¶ЛгБЯгГЗгГЉгВњгВТзЫіжО•гВ≥гГФгГЉгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБ™гБПгАБгВҐгГЉгГИгВДйЯ≥ж•љгАБгГЖгВ≠гВєгГИгБ™гБ©гАБжЦ∞гБЧгБДгВВгБЃгВТжГ≥еГПгБЧгБ¶зФЯжИРгБЩгВЛгВИгБЖгБ™гВВгБЃгБІгБЩгАВ
зФЯжИРAIгБЃеЯЇжЬђгВТе≠¶гБґгБЂгБѓгАБжЬђгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБЃеЯЇгБ®гБ™гВЛиЛ±и™ЮзЙИиСЧиАЕгБМеЕђйЦЛгБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛгАМGenerative AI for BeginnersгАНгВТгБФи¶ІгБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ
AIгАБж©ЯжҐ∞е≠¶зњТпЉИMLпЉЙгАБзФЯжИРAIгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶йЭЮеЄЄгБЂгВЈгГ≥гГЧгГЂгБ™ељҐгБІзРЖиІ£гБЧгБЯгБДе†іеРИгБѓгАБеРМгБШгБПжЬђгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБЃиЛ±и™ЮзЙИиСЧиАЕгБМеЕђйЦЛгБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛгАМHow I Explained AI, Machine Learning and Generative AI to My 5 Year-old KidгАНгВТгБФеПВзЕІгБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВ

 

AI CopilotгБ®гБѓпЉЯ

гБ°гВЗгБЖгБ©еЙѓжУНзЄ¶е£ЂгБМгГСгВ§гГ≠гГГгГИгВТи£ЬдљРгБЧгБ¶й£Ыи°Мж©ЯгВТжУНзЄ¶гБЩгВЛгВИгБЖгБЂгАБAI CopilotгБѓжІШгАЕгБ™гВњгВєгВѓгБІгБВгБ™гБЯгВТеК©гБСгВЛгВєгГЮгГЉгГИгБ™гВҐгВЈгВєгВњгГ≥гГИгБ®гБЧгБ¶ж©ЯиГљгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ
AI CopilotгБѓдЇЇеЈ•зЯ•иГљгВТжіїзФ®гБЧгБ¶гБВгБ™гБЯгБЃгГЛгГЉгВЇгВТзРЖиІ£гБЧгАБжПРж°ИгВДгВµгГЭгГЉгГИгВТжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛгГДгГЉгГЂгБІгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБЂгВИгВКгАБдљЬж•≠гБМгВИгВКз∞°еНШгБІеКєзОЗзЪДгБЂгБ™гВКгБЊгБЩгАВ

AI CopilotгБѓгБ©гБЃгВИгБЖгБЂеЛХдљЬгБЩгВЛгБЃгБІгБЧгВЗгБЖгБЛпЉЯ

жКАи°УзЪДгБЂгБѓгАБAI CopilotгБѓе§Іи¶Пж®°и®Аи™ЮгГҐгГЗгГЂпЉИLLM: Large Language ModelпЉЙгВТдљњзФ®гБЧгБ¶гАБгГ¶гГЉгВґгГЉгВТжІШгАЕгБ™гВњгВєгВѓгВДжДПжАЭж±ЇеЃЪгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБІгВµгГЭгГЉгГИгБЩгВЛдЉЪи©±еЮЛгВ§гГ≥гВњгГЉгГХгВІгГЉгВєгБІгБЩгАВ
LLMгВТжіїзФ®гБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБAI CopilotгБѓиЖ®е§ІгБ™йЗПгБЃгГЗгГЉгВњгВТзРЖиІ£гГїеИЖжЮРгГїеЗ¶зРЖгБЩгВЛиГљеКЫгВТжМБгБ£гБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ
з∞°еНШгБ™дЊЛгВТжМЩгБТгБЊгБЩгАВдЊЛгБИгБ∞гАБгБВгБ™гБЯгБМPCгБІгГђгГЭгГЉгГИгВТжЫЄгБДгБ¶гБДгВЛгБ®гБЧгБЊгБЩгАВAI CopilotгБѓгАБгБВгБ™гБЯгБМжЫЄгБДгБ¶гБДгВЛеЖЕеЃєгВТзРЖиІ£гБЧгАБгВИгВКиЙѓгБДи®АиСЙгВДи°®зПЊгВТжПРж°ИгБЧгБЯгВКгАБгВєгГЪгГЂгГБгВІгГГгВѓгВДжЦЗж≥ХгБЃдњЃж≠£гВТи°МгБ£гБЯгВКгАБгГђгГЭгГЉгГИгБЂеРЂгВБгВЛгБєгБНйЦҐйА£жГЕе†±гВТи¶ЛгБ§гБСгВЛжЙЛеК©гБСгВТгБЧгБ¶гБПгВМгВЛгГДгГЉгГЂгБ®гБЧгБ¶ж©ЯиГљгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ
и¶БгБЩгВЛгБЂгАБAI CopilotгБѓи§ЗйЫСгБ™гВњгВєгВѓгВТз∞°зі†еМЦгБЧгАБи≤ійЗНгБ™гВђгВ§гГАгГ≥гВєгБ®гВµгГЭгГЉгГИгВТжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБІгАБжЬАзµВзЪДгБЂгБѓгГ¶гГЉгВґгГЉгБЃдљУй®УгВТеРСдЄКгБХгБЫгАБгГУгВЄгГНгВєгБМеКєжЮЬзЪДгБЛгБ§еКєзОЗзЪДгБЂзЫЃж®ЩгБЂеРСгБЛгБЖгБУгБ®гВТжФѓжПігБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ

еЄВе†ігБЂгБКгБСгВЛгБДгБПгБ§гБЛгБЃAI Copilot

JouleгБЃдїЦгБЂгВВгАБдї•дЄЛгБЃгВИгБЖгБ™AI CopilotгВµгГЉгГУгВєгБМе±ХйЦЛгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

гГїMicrosoft Copilot
MS Officeи£љеУБгБ®йА£жРЇгБЧгАБWordгГїExcelгГїPowerPointгБ™гБ©гБЃгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЂгБКгБСгВЛгВµгГЭгГЉгГИгВТжПРдЊЫ

гГїGitHub Copilot
гВИгВКеКєзОЗзЪДгБЛгБ§еКєжЮЬзЪДгБ™гВ≥гГЉгГЙйЦЛзЩЇгВТжФѓжПі

Data Contextualization (гГЗгГЉгВњгБЃжЦЗиДИеМЦ)гБ®гБѓпЉЯ

Data Contextualization (гГЗгГЉгВњгБЃжЦЗиДИеМЦ) гБ®гБѓгАБгГЗгГЉгВњгБЂйЦҐйА£гБЩгВЛжГЕе†±гВТињљеК†гБЧгБ¶гАБгВИгВКеЃЯзФ®зЪДгБ™гВВгБЃгБЂгБЩгВЛгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБІгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБЂгБѓгАБиГМжЩѓжГЕе†±гВДгГСгВњгГЉгГ≥гАБгГИгГђгГ≥гГЙгАБгВҐгВ¶гГИгГ©гВ§гГ§гГЉпЉИе§ЦгВМеА§пЉЙгБ™гБ©гВТеРЂгВБгАБгГЗгГЉгВњгБМдљХгВТжДПеС≥гБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛгБЃгБЛгГ¶гГЉгВґгБМзРЖиІ£гБЧгВДгБЩгБПгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЃжЙЛж≥ХгБМеРЂгБЊгВМгБЊгБЩгАВ
гБЭгВМгБІгБѓгБУгБУгБЛгВЙгАБжЬђгГЦгГ≠гВ∞гБЃгГ°гВ§гГ≥гГЖгГЉгГЮгБІгБВгВЛJouleгБЂгБ§гБДгБ¶и¶ЛгБ¶гБДгБНгБЊгБЧгВЗгБЖпЉБ

 

жФєгВБгБ¶гАБJouleгБ®гБѓпЉЯ

дњ°й†ЉгБІгБНгВЛAIгГСгГЉгГИгГКгГЉгБМгБВгБ™гБЯгБЃгБЭгБ∞гБЂгБДгБ¶гАБиЗ™зДґи®Аи™ЮгБЃзРЖиІ£гГїзФЯжИРиГљеКЫгВТжіїзФ®гБЧгАБSAPгБЃгВѓгГ©гВ¶гГЙгГЭгГЉгГИгГХгВ©гГ™гВ™еЕ®дљУгБІжЧ•гАЕгБЃж•≠еЛЩгВТжФѓжПігГїеРСдЄКгБХгБЫгБ¶гБПгВМгВЛе≠ШеЬ®гАБгБЭгВМгБМJouleгБІгБЩгАВ

JouleгБѓжђ°гБЃгВИгБЖгБ™зЙєеЊігВТжМБгБ£гБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩпЉЪ

  • еЕИйА≤зЪДгБ™зФЯжИРAI Copilot
  • SAPгБЃгВѓгГ©гВ¶гГЙгВ®гГ≥гВњгГЉгГЧгГ©гВ§гВЇгГЭгГЉгГИгГХгВ©гГ™гВ™еЕ®дљУгБЂEmbeddedгБХгВМгБЯ(еЯЛгВБиЊЉгБЊгВМгБЯ)е≠ШеЬ®
  • SAPгБЃгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВДгВµгГЉгГЙгГСгГЉгГЖгВ£гБЃгВљгГЉгВєгБЛгВЙеєЕеЇГгБПжЈ±гБДжГЕе†±гВТеПЦгВКиЊЉгБњгАБиГљеЛХзЪДгБЛгБ§жЦЗиДИгБЂеЯЇгБ•гБДгБЯгВ§гГ≥гВµгВ§гГИгВТжПРдЊЫ

JouleгБѓгАБдЇЇгАЕгБМгВИгВКињЕйАЯгБЂдїХдЇЛгВТйА≤гВБгАБеЃЙеЕ®гБЛгБ§гВ≥гГ≥гГЧгГ©гВ§гВҐгГ≥гВєгБЂжЇЦжЛ†гБЧгБЯжЦєж≥ХгБІгВИгВКиЙѓгБДгГУгВЄгГНгВєжИРжЮЬгВТдЄКгБТгВЛгБЃгВТжФѓжПігБЩгВЛгВИгБЖи®≠и®ИгБХгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

гБКеЃҐжІШгБЃJouleжіїзФ®гВ§гГ°гГЉгВЄ

JouleгБѓи§ЗжХ∞гБЃгВЈгВєгГЖгГ†гБЛгВЙгГЗгГЉгВњгВТињЕйАЯгБЂжХізРЖгБЧгАБжЦЗиДИгВТињљеК†гБЧгБ¶гВєгГЮгГЉгГИгБ™гВ§гГ≥гВµгВ§гГИгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ
JouleгБѓгАБдЇЇдЇЛгАБи≤°еЛЩгАБгВµгГЧгГ©гВ§гГБгВІгГЉгГ≥гАБи™њйБФгАБй°ІеЃҐдљУй®УгБ™гБ©гБЃй†ШеЯЯгБЂйЦҐгБЩгВЛSAPгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гАБгБКгВИгБ≥SAP BTP: Business Technology PlatformгБЂзµДгБњиЊЉгБЊгВМгБЊгБЩгАВJouleгБѓгАБSAPгБЃгГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгБКгВИгБ≥гГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгБІдЇЛеЙНгБЂе≠¶зњТгБМи°МгВПгВМгБ¶гБДгБЊгБЩгАВ
гГ¶гГЉгВґгГЉгБѓеНШгБЂи≥™еХПгВТгБЧгБЯгВКгАБеХПй°МгВТеє≥жШУгБ™и®АиСЙгБІињ∞гБєгВЛгБ†гБСгБІгАБSAPгБЃгГЭгГЉгГИгГХгВ©гГ™гВ™гБКгВИгБ≥гВµгГЉгГЙгГСгГЉгГЖгВ£гВљгГЉгВєеЕ®дљУгБЛгВЙиЖ®е§ІгБ™гГУгВЄгГНгВєгГЗгГЉгВњгВТеЯЇгБЂгБЧгБЯгВ§гГ≥гГЖгГ™гВЄгВІгГ≥гГИгБ™еЫЮз≠ФгВТгАБжЦЗиДИгВТзґ≠жМБгБЧгБЯгБЊгБЊеПЧгБСеПЦгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ

 

дЊЛгБИгБ∞гАБи£љйА†ж•≠иАЕгБМи≤©е£≤еЃЯзЄЊгВТгВИгВКиЙѓгБПзРЖиІ£гБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгБЂJouleгБЂгГШгГЂгГЧгВТж±ВгВБгВЛгВЈгГБгГ•гВ®гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гВТжГ≥еГПгБЧгБ¶гБњгБ¶гБПгБ†гБХгБДгАВJouleгБѓи≤©е£≤гБМдљОињЈгБЧгБ¶гБДгВЛеЬ∞еЯЯгВТзЙєеЃЪгБЧгАБгВµгГЧгГ©гВ§гГБгВІгГЉгГ≥гБЃеХПй°МгВТжШОгВЙгБЛгБЂгБЩгВЛдїЦгБЃгГЗгГЉгВњгВїгГГгГИгБ®гГ™гГ≥гВѓгБЧгАБгБЭгБЃеЊМгАБгВµгГЧгГ©гВ§гГБгВІгГЉгГ≥гВЈгВєгГЖгГ†гБЂиЗ™еЛХзЪДгБЂжО•зґЪгБЧгБ¶и£љйА†ж•≠иАЕгБМж§Ьи®ОгБІгБНгВЛжљЬеЬ®зЪДгБ™еѓЊз≠ЦгВТжПРж°ИгБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВ
гБЊгБЯгАБHRпЉИдЇЇдЇЛпЉЙгБЃеИЖйЗОгБІгБѓгАБJouleгБѓеБПгВКгБЃгБ™гБДж±ВдЇЇжГЕе†±гВТдљЬжИРгБЧгБЯгВКгАБйБ©еИЗгБ™йЭҐжО•и≥™еХПгВТзФЯжИРгБЩгВЛгБЃгБЂгВВељєзЂЛгБ°гБЊгБЩгАВ

 

JouleгБѓSAP Conversational AIгБЃеЊМзґЩгБІгБЩгБЛпЉЯ

жШОзҐЇгБЂи®АгБЖгБ®гАБJouleгБѓSAP Conversational AIгБЃеЊМзґЩгБ®гБЧгБ¶дљНзљЃгБ•гБСгВЙгВМгВЛгВПгБСгБІгБѓгБ™гБПгАБгВАгБЧгВНйА≤еМЦељҐгВДи£ЬеЃМзЪДгБ™е≠ШеЬ®гБ®иАГгБИгВЛгБУгБ®гБМгБІгБНгБЊгБЩгАВSAP Conversational AIгБѓдЄїгБЂгГБгГ£гГГгГИгГЬгГГгГИгВДиЗ™зДґи®Аи™ЮеЗ¶зРЖгБЂгГХгВ©гГЉгВЂгВєгБЧгБЯгГДгГЉгГЂгБІгБЩгБМгАБJouleгБѓгБЭгВМгВТеРЂгВБгБ¶гАБгБХгВЙгБЂйЂШеЇ¶гБ™зФЯжИРAIгВДгГЗгГЉгВњгБЃжЦЗиДИеМЦж©ЯиГљгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВ
JouleгБѓгАБSAPгБЃгВѓгГ©гВ¶гГЙгВ®гГ≥гВњгГЉгГЧгГ©гВ§гВЇгГЭгГЉгГИгГХгВ©гГ™гВ™еЕ®дљУгБЂгВПгБЯгВЛгГЗгГЉгВњгВТеЯЇгБЂгАБгГЧгГ≠гВҐгВѓгГЖгВ£гГЦгБЛгБ§жЦЗиДИгБЂеЯЇгБ•гБДгБЯгВ§гГ≥гВµгВ§гГИгВТжПРдЊЫгБЩгВЛгБЯгВБгАБгВИгВКеЇГзѓДгБ™ж•≠еЛЩгГЧгГ≠гВїгВєгВТжФѓжПігБЩгВЛгБУгБ®гБМеПѓиГљгБІгБЩгАВгБ§гБЊгВКгАБJouleгБѓSAP Conversational AIгБЃж©ЯиГљгВТиґЕгБИгБ¶гАБгГ¶гГЉгВґгГЉгБЂгБ®гБ£гБ¶гБХгВЙгБЂе§ЪгБПгБЃдЊ°еА§гВТзФЯгБњеЗЇгБЩжђ°дЄЦдї£гБЃAI CopilotгБ®и®АгБИгВЛгБІгБЧгВЗгБЖгАВ

 

гБКгБХгБИгБ¶гБКгБПгБєгБНJouleгБЃйЗНи¶БдЇЛй†Е

JouleгБЃйЗНи¶БдЇЛй†ЕгБѓдї•дЄЛгБЃ3зВєгБІгБЩгАВ

SAPгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБ®гБЃзµ±еРИ

JouleгБѓгАБSAPгВҐгГЧгГ™гВ±гГЉгВЈгГІгГ≥гБЂзµ±еРИгБХгВМгБЯдЉЪи©±еЮЛгГ¶гГЉгВґгГЉгВ§гГ≥гВњгГЉгГХгВІгГЉгВєгВТжПРдЊЫгБЧгБЊгБЩгАВгБУгВМгБѓгАБSAP FioriжЇЦжЛ†гБЃUIгВ≥гГ≥гГИгГ≠гГЉгГЂгВТдљњзФ®гБЧгБ¶гВҐгВЈгВєгВњгГ≥гГИгБЃењЬз≠ФгВТи°®з§ЇгБЩгВЛгГ™гГГгГБгБ™гВ¶гВІгГЦгВѓгГ©гВ§гВҐгГ≥гГИгБІгБЩгАВ

гВ®гГ≥гВњгГЉгГЧгГ©гВ§гВЇеѓЊењЬ

entry.title: Peppa's Secret Language of SAP , Data and AI: Matching Business Puzzles with AI Algorithm Pieces
entry.content:
No More Square Pegs: Conquering Business Challenges with the Right Algorithm Formula

Peppa Pig, despite her love for muddy puddles, was a dab hand at solving problems. So, when Mummy Pig's online shop, "Pretty Oinkments," started losing its sparkle, Peppa knew she had to help.

"We need to use SAP BTP Business AI Launchpad, Peppa!" declared Mummy Pig, tapping away at her computer. " Launchpad models and Algorithms are the answer!"

Peppa, though eager to try anything new, knew that simply throwing algorithms at the problem was like throwing a tantrum вАУ messy and ineffective.

One afternoon, while puzzling over charts and graphs, she remembered a saying from Madame Gazelle

"Data is like a block of marble. An algorithm is the chisel. But you need a vision of the sculpture you want to create before you start chiseling."

That's when it clicked! Peppa had been so focused on the algorithms that she'd forgotten to ask the most important question: what were they trying to bake?

She put on her thinking cap and considered:

  1. What was the real problem - "Was it just fewer orders, or were customers ignoring the new line of "Glitter & Glow" hoof polish?"

  2. What insights did they need from their data? - "Should they find out which piggies preferred bubble bath over belly rubs?"

With a clearer vision, Peppa realized she needed a special algorithm recipe:

 Clustering to group piggies with similar oinkment preferences.
Association Rule Mining to see what products were often bought together (perhaps mud masks and marigold shampoo?).
Time Series Analysis to predict demand for seasonal favorites like "Sun's Out, Snouts Out" sunscreen.

By choosing the right algorithms based on their specific needs, Peppa and Mummy Pig turned things around. Orders poured in, customer happiness soared, and "Pretty Oinkments" became the most stylish online shop for pigs far and wide.

Peppa learned a valuable lesson: AI algorithms are powerful, but they're only as good as the questions you ask of your data.

Choose wisely, and you'll whip up a recipe for success. Choose blindly, and you'll be left with a digital mess вАУ even messier than a muddy puddle!

Think of your business challenges as puzzles waiting to be solved. Artificial intelligence (AI) offers a powerful set of algorithms, each like a unique puzzle piece, ready to unlock solutions. But how do you choose the right piece for the right puzzle? Think of this blog as your AI decoder ring, helping you match common business challenges to their perfect algorithmic solutions.

Beyond Buzzwords: Matching Algorithms to Real-World Business Problems

1. The Automation Enigma: Streamlining Repetitive Tasks

Problem Pieces: Tedious data entry, manual invoice processing, time-consuming report generation, repetitive scheduling nightmares.

AI Puzzle Solvers

Robotic Process Automation (RPA): Imagine a tireless digital assistant processing insurance claims. RPA bots can extract data from forms, update systems, and generate reports вАУ all without human intervention, freeing up your team for more strategic work.

Decision Trees: Let's say your online store has a rule: free shipping on orders over $50. A decision tree can automatically apply this rule at checkout, ensuring consistency and efficiency. These algorithms excel at automating simple, rule-based processes.

2. The Prediction Puzzle: Peering into the Future of Your Business

Problem Pieces: Forecasting sales trends, predicting customer churn, anticipating website traffic spikes, assessing financial risks.

AI Puzzle Solvers

Regression Analysis: Imagine predicting the optimal price for a new product launch. By analyzing historical sales data, competitor pricing, and market trends, a regression model can forecast sales at different price points, helping you maximize revenue.

Time Series Analysis: Picture a dashboard predicting website traffic during a major marketing campaign. By analyzing historical traffic patterns, seasonality, and promotional calendars, a time series model can forecast traffic surges, ensuring your website can handle the load.

Neural Networks: Let's say you want to detect fraudulent credit card transactions. A neural network can learn subtle patterns of fraudulent behavior from vast datasets, flagging suspicious transactions in real-time to prevent financial losses.

Non-Time Based Regression: Imagine predicting the energy consumption of a building based on factors like square footage, occupancy, and weather data. This type of regression model can help optimize energy usage and reduce costs.

3. The Customer Insight Puzzle: Unlocking the Minds of Your Audience

Problem Pieces: Identifying customer segments, personalising marketing messages, recommending relevant products, understanding customer sentiment.

AI Puzzle Solvers:

Clustering: Imagine segmenting your customer base based on purchase history, website browsing behavior, and social media interactions. By grouping similar customers, you can tailor marketing campaigns, personalize offers, and boost engagement.

Association Rule Mining: Think of Amazon's "Customers who bought this item also bought..." recommendations. Association rule mining can uncover similar relationships within your sales data, suggesting relevant products to customers at the right moment, increasing sales and customer satisfaction.

Recommendation Models: Picture a music streaming service suggesting songs you'll love. By analyzing your listening history, preferences, and songs enjoyed by similar users, a recommendation model can curate a personalized listening experience, keeping you engaged and coming back for more.

4. The Efficiency Puzzle: Optimizing Operations for Maximum Impact

Problem Pieces: Streamlining logistics and delivery routes, optimizing inventory levels across warehouses, detecting anomalies in financial transactions.

AI Puzzle Solvers:

Genetic Algorithms: Imagine a ride-sharing app optimizing routes for drivers. A genetic algorithm can analyze traffic patterns, rider demand, and driver locations to continuously evolve and find the most efficient routes in real-time, minimizing wait times and maximizing earnings.

Reinforcement Learning: Picture a smart energy grid dynamically adjusting energy distribution based on real-time demand and supply. Reinforcement learning algorithms can learn from past decisions and adapt to changing conditions, optimizing energy usage and grid stability.

5. The Dimensionality Reduction Puzzle: Simplifying Complex Data Landscapes

Problem Pieces: Dealing with datasets containing hundreds or thousands of variables, making analysis and pattern recognition difficult.

AI Puzzle Solvers:

Principal Component Analysis (PCA): Imagine analyzing customer feedback with hundreds of data points. PCA can reduce this complexity by identifying the most important factors driving customer satisfaction, making it easier to focus on key areas for improvement.

Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA): Let's say you want to predict which patients are at the highest risk of developing a certain disease based on their medical history and genetic data. LDA can pinpoint the most influential factors, enabling early intervention and personalized treatment plans.

6. The Ranking Puzzle: Prioritizing Actions for Maximum Impact

Problem Pieces: Ranking sales leads based on their likelihood to convert, prioritizing customer service tickets based on urgency, identifying the most influential factors driving customer churn.

AI Puzzle Solvers:

RankSVM: Imagine a news website personalizing content recommendations. RankSVM can analyze your reading history and preferences to prioritize articles you're most likely to find interesting, keeping you engaged and coming back for more.

LambdaMART: Think of a job board matching candidates with relevant job postings. LambdaMART can analyze candidate profiles, skills, and experience to rank them based on their suitability for specific jobs, improving matching efficiency and candidate satisfaction.

Even Peppa Pig wants to hear your thoughts! Share your AI algorithm wins (and fails) in the comments. :)!

entry.title: Navigating SAP's AI Ecosystem: A Strategic Guide for Finance and Procurement Leaders
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, artificial intelligence has moved beyond being a technological novelty to become a critical driver of operational excellence and competitive advantage. For finance, procurement, and accounting leaders, understanding how to leverage SAP's comprehensive AI portfolio effectively can be the key to unlocking unprecedented efficiency and insights within their organizations.

 

Understanding SAP's AI Portfolio

SAP has structured its AI offerings into three distinct categories, each designed to address different organizational needs and readiness levels:

Base AI represents the foundation of SAP's intelligent enterprise vision. These embedded machine learning features come included with your existing subscription, offering immediate value without additional investment. These capabilities are already woven into your day-to-day operations, enhancing processes from automated invoice matching to predictive analytics for cash flow management.

Premium AI elevates your capabilities with advanced generative AI solutions. These sophisticated tools require AI unitsвАФSAP's measurement of computing power consumptionвАФand can be seamlessly integrated into your existing workflows. Think of AI units as the fuel that powers these advanced features, with consumption varying based on the complexity and scale of your use cases across SAP's solution portfolio.

Joule, SAP's AI agent, represents the newest frontier in enterprise AI assistance. Available with a free subscription up to certain usage thresholds, Joule serves as your intelligent business companion, capable of handling complex queries and automating sophisticated workflows across your financial operations.

 

Strategic Implementation: A Practical Approach

To maximize the value of SAP's AI capabilities while ensuring responsible resource allocation, we recommend following a structured approach to implementation:

First and foremost, begin with a clear business need. The most successful AI implementations enhance already robust processes rather than attempting to fix broken ones. Take time to articulate specific challenges within your financial or procurement operations where AI could drive meaningful improvements.

Next, explore SAP's extensive catalog of AI features through the SAP Discovery Center Business AI Features portal (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog/?category=pp.5). This comprehensive resource allows you to match your business needs with existing capabilities, potentially saving significant time and resources in custom development.

Once you've identified promising features, engage with your Customer Success Manager or Account Executive. These professionals can provide detailed insights into implementation requirements, AI unit needs for Premium features, and ROI calculations tailored to your use case. To better understand the resource requirements, utilize the AI Feature Estimator (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-estimator) for accurate projections of AI unit consumption and associated costs.

When existing features don't precisely match your needs, consider these alternatives:

1. Joule Integration: Explore how SAP's AI agent can be deployed to address your specific use case, taking advantage of the free subscription tier to pilot the solution.

2. Custom Development: The SAP Business Technology Platform offers a robust foundation for building customized AI solutions. Access the full catalog of AI services through the BTP AI services catalog (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/servicessearch/AI%20Enabled) and explore existing AI missions (https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missionCatalog/) that might align with your objectives.

3. Partner Solutions: The SAP Store (https://www.sap.com/store.html) showcases AI solutions developed by certified partners, offering pre-built solutions that might address your needs without requiring custom development.

 

Making the Right Choice

Success in implementing AI within your financial operations depends on careful evaluation of your needs, resources, and objectives. Consider the following factors:

Implementation Timeline: Base AI features offer immediate value with minimal setup, while Premium AI solutions might require more extensive preparation but deliver more sophisticated capabilities.

Resource Requirements: Factor in both the initial investment and ongoing AI unit consumption for Premium AI features. The AI Feature Estimator provides essential insights for accurate cost projections and resource planning.

ROI Potential: Work with your SAP representatives to develop detailed ROI calculations for each potential implementation, ensuring your AI investments align with business objectives.

 

Next Steps

Begin your AI journey by:

1. Documenting specific challenges within your financial or procurement processes

2. Reviewing available features in the SAP Discovery Center

3. Scheduling a consultation with your SAP representative to discuss potential implementations

Remember, the goal is not to implement AI for its own sake but to drive meaningful improvements in your operations. Take advantage of demonstration sessions and pilot programs to ensure each implementation delivers tangible value to your organization.

 

For more information about specific features or to begin your AI implementation journey, visit the SAP Discovery Center's AI catalog or contact your SAP representative. The resources linked throughout this article provide comprehensive guidance for each stage of your AI implementation journey.

entry.title: GenAI features self-activation step by step with screenshots
entry.content:
Did you know that system administrators can turn on SAP SuccessFactors GenAI features themselves once 2 simple prerequisites are met ? In this blog we cover prerequisites, RBP steps (with screenshots) and AI Units consumption explanations for a successful start with SAP SuccessFactors GenAI features.

 

Prerequisites

  • Customers must have purchased SKU 8016532 вАЬSAP AI UnitsвАЭ through their Account Executive (SAP internal note: navigate to Cloud Reporting then search for SKU number which should appear if purchased). Customers must have also signed the T&Cs with their AE. Be aware that there are other SKU with a different number that have вАЬAI UnitsвАЭ in their title but only SKU 8016532 will allow GenAI features to work properly.
  • The environment where GenAI is about to be permissioned/activated must have the checkboxes below checked in Provisioning > Company settings (this can only be done by SAP Provisioning teams). SAP internal note: If a few days after purchasing AI Units with SKU 8016532 the below checkboxes still arenвАЩt checked then escalation must be made by email to SF Provisioning team (or reach out to me or anyone from SAP AI Adoption team (Leads: NA: Parul Luthra / EMEA: Jenny Geipel / APJ: Adarsh Mahalingappa). Please also note that the latest version of each module is required for GenAI to work.
    • Enable AI-Assisted SAP SuccessFactors HCM Content Generator
    • Enable AI-Assisted SAP SuccessFactors HCM Skills

 

Step 1

  • Access "Manage Permissions Roles"
  • Grant "Manage AI Capabilities" to Super Admin role

  • Log out / Log back in

 

Step 2

  • In Search for Actions type вАЬAI Services AdministrationвАЭ page then turn on AI features as per screenshot below. If you donвАЩt see вАЬAI Services AdministrationвАЭ in Search for Actions  after several hours then have SAP Support or a certified consultant run a RBP Refresh from Provisioning > Company Settings.

  • Log out / Log back in 

 

Step 3

  • In "Manage Permission Roles" find the "Employee on Self" role (or create a new one for only some employees/testers) then in вАЬUser PermissionsвАЭ select вАЬAI AccessвАЭ. If you donвАЩt see вАЬAI AccessвАЭ section even after several hours then have SAP Support or a certified consultant run a RBP Refresh from Provisioning > Company Settings.
  • Grant permissions for the right AI feature by checking the right boxes.

 

  • Log out / Log back in
  • Proxy as an employee who is part of the role you granted AI Access permission for and you can start testing Gen AI features.

 

How to track consumption

  • Consumption dashboards in SAP For Me get updated overnight after the first use of AI features and show AI Units consumption from all environments where Gen AI features are used (any environment with GenAI features being used is billable). The dashboards only show GenAI features consumption of AI Units, not Joule (Joule consumption of AI Units is only available at this time through a report that can be requested by customers to their SAP CSM).
  • More information on the SAP For Me > Business AI dashboards can be found in this video: https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_dahk6ib7
  • If customer is still seeing an error message in the SAP For Me > Business AI dashboard even a few days after activating GenAI features please see this KBA for possible explanations and create an incident to support. 
  • To understand AI Units consumption better, please see in the screenshot below the concept of 1 A4 page = 0.007 AI Units. To find out how many words are considered in an A4 page we advise customers to copy paste a sample job description from their environment into a A4 Word document with standard margins and line spacing = 0 (copy the job description several time until bottom of A4 page is reached) then use the word count feature.

 

  • Joule message consumption вАУ Is it separate than AI Units ? 
    • AI Units are allocated separately as part of Joule vs GenAI activation but their consumption is connected as per example below:
      • As of 1/23/2025 when Joule is activated AI Units are allocated to each customer at no cost through SKU 8017178 based on number of users in the SuccessFactors environment.
      • In order to turn on GenAI features customers must purchase AI Units through SKU 8016532 (prerequisite)
      • If customers that have both Joule and GenAI use all AI Units allocated through SKU 8017178 (Joule) then SAP will take from remaining AI Units allocated through SKU 8017718 (Gen AI) for billing purposes.

 

Additional links: 

  • Artificial Intelligence in SAP Success Factors Guide for Activation вАУPremium AI Use Cases (ppt presentation)
  • Premium AI Features in SAP SuccessFactors (Implementation guide)
  • SuccessFactors AI - AI-assisted writing and writing - translation (Set up and hints)
  • Unlock the Power of SAP HCM GenAI: Discover Use Cases, Pre-requisites, and RBP Requirements

 

All the best,

Xavier



(If you found this blog useful please consider giving it a Like)
